title: #eng-sre: 1771997007
preview:
Dev Patel: Hey @Sean Gallagher @Rafael Mendes — can you take a quick look at the proposed Support SLA table before we publish? Main question: does this align w/ paging thresholds + error budget policy. Draft: https://confluence.redwood.ai/x/support-sla-policy-draft

Dev Patel: TL;DR targets in draft:
- Enterprise P0: first response 15m, updates every 30m
- Enterprise P1: first response 1h, updates every 2h
- Dedicated P0: first response 30m, updates every 1h
- Hosted self-serve: best-effort within business hours

Sean Gallagher: Looking now. First reaction: the “updates every 30m” language for Enterprise P0 is fine for *comms*, but the escalation trigger column doesn’t match how we actually page / declare incidents.

Sean Gallagher: Example mismatch: draft says “page SRE for P0 if >5% error rate for 10m OR >3 customers impacted”. Our SLO alerting/paging policy is still “global availability SLO burn” and hard paging thresholds are higher for some routes (and lower for others).

Rachel Kim: +1 on comms vs paging. For Enterprise, we can commit to update cadence even if Engineering isn’t paging yet, but we need a clean trigger for when Support should ask for SRE involvement.

Rafael Mendes: Yeah, right now if Support pages on “3 customers impacted” we’ll get spammed during noisy-but-contained issues. We try to page on customer impact *plus* platform signal (burn rate, regional degradation, elevated 5xx across multiple tenants).

Allison Grant: Also worth calling out error budget policy: we don’t page on every blip; we page when SLO burn indicates risk of violating monthly budget / sustained degradation. The doc reads like a static threshold table.

Dev Patel: Makes sense. What would you change specifically?

Sean Gallagher: Proposed edits:
1) Separate “Support SLA” (response + customer update cadence) from “Engineering paging thresholds” (SRE involvement)
2) For escalation triggers, reference the SLO burn alerts + a simplified decision tree Support can apply.

Sean Gallagher: Something like:
- P0/Sev0 candidate: complete outage OR sustained severe degradation (multi-tenant or single enterprise w/ SLA) AND no workaround
- Page SRE if (a) status page criteria met OR (b) SLO burn alerts firing (link) OR (c) enterprise dedicated/private cannot mitigate w/ config/workaround within 30m

Rafael Mendes: + add a note: “Support can start a bridge anytime for coordination, but paging SRE requires either SLO signal or clear blast radius.”

Chris Osei: From Product Ops side, we should be careful not to imply contractual “engineering response times.” Keep it framed as Support comms + internal escalation guidance.

Rachel Kim: Re: update cadence — can we explicitly say “every 30m while actively investigating/mitigating; if Waiting on Customer, cadence pauses but we send a handoff note”? We keep getting ambiguity there.

Sean Gallagher: Yes. But please don’t word it as “SLA clock pauses” unless it’s tied to Jira automation. Better: “Update cadence applies while Redwood is actively working; if blocked on customer action, we’ll send a summary + next needed info.”

Dev Patel: Got it. Another question: does the draft’s trigger “>5% errors for 10m” conflict with error budget policy?

Allison Grant: It can. A flat 5% for 10m might not burn enough budget for some low-volume endpoints, and might be too lax for high-volume ones. Better to point to burn rate alert + dashboard quick view.

Rafael Mendes: Yep. If Support wants a simple rule, use: “if customer sees widespread 5xx/429 across tenants in a region AND dashboards show elevated error rate, start bridge + page on-call.” But still link the canonical alerts.

Sean Gallagher: Concrete action items I’d suggest for the doc:
- Replace numeric thresholds w/ “SLO burn alert firing” and “region/service health dashboard indicates sustained degradation”
- Add link: support-sla-vs-engineering-slo-mapping (Confluence)
- Add “do not page” examples (single tenant misconfig, quota confusion, one SDK version bug w/ workaround)

Dev Patel: Makes sense. Can you drop the exact links you want referenced?

Sean Gallagher: Link these:
- https://confluence.redwood.ai/x/support-sla-vs-eng-slo
- https://confluence.redwood.ai/x/slo-alerts-and-burn-rates
- (and the incident process overview) https://confluence.redwood.ai/x/incident-process

Rachel Kim: Also: for Enterprise P0 updates every 30m is ok, but we should add “if we move to #incidents, status page comms follow incident comms policy.” Otherwise Support will send an email that conflicts w/ status page language.

Dev Patel: Noted. I’ll update the table to split columns:
- “Support SLA targets”
- “Escalation trigger (Support -> Eng)” referencing SLO/burn + blast radius

Rafael Mendes: One more: please add “don’t bypass incident commander.” If it’s a Sev0, Support should ask for IC assignment instead of running the bridge themselves.

Dev Patel: Yep. I’ll add: “If criteria met for Sev0/Sev1, move to #incidents and request IC; Support owns customer updates, IC owns internal cadence.”

Sean Gallagher: :thumbsup: Once you update, tag me again. Happy to sign off.

Dev Patel: Will do. Thanks all.

title: #eng-sre: 1771745501
preview:
PagerDuty: [ALERT] suggestions-api p95 latency regression
Service: optimize-suggestions
Endpoint: GET /optimize/suggestions
p95: 1.42s (baseline 320ms) for 10m
error rate: 0.6% (mostly 504s at edge)
Region: us-east-1
Dash: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/opt-suggest/api-latency
Runbook: https://confluence.redwood.ai/x/runbook-suggestions-api-latency-and-error-triage
:rotating_light:

Sean Gallagher: looking. anyone else seeing correlating deploys? this started ~10-15m ago on the chart.

Aisha Bello: yep, p95 + p99 both spiking. cpu on suggestions-api pods not crazy, but DB time jumped hard.

Allison Grant: also seeing increased DB connections in pg dashboard. feels like query regression rather than infra.

Sean Gallagher: checking deploy feed… we had a rollout to optimize-suggestions around ~30m ago? @Dylan Brooks @Chloe Martin do we have a change touching list query/pagination?

Dylan Brooks: Console side had a minor UI change behind flag but shouldn’t affect API. backend had a deploy earlier today. looping in @Felix Schneider who touched the endpoint last.

Chloe Martin: I’m on. looking at trace sample now. majority of time in `SuggestionRepository.listForProject` and then a burst of `SELECT ... FROM suggestions_inputs` per row.

Felix Schneider: on it. that sounds like N+1. we recently added a “stale_as_of” + extra explanation fields and I may have accidentally triggered lazy-load per suggestion.

Sean Gallagher: can we confirm if this is only when `include_details=true` or also base list?

Aisha Bello: both. base list call is now pulling details payload even without drawer opened (per trace tags). might be server default.

Felix Schneider: ugh. I see it. we changed default serializer to include `inputs_summary` always. it hits `suggestions_inputs` table per suggestion row.

Allison Grant: mitigation options:
1) rollback last deploy
2) hotfix to remove details join / make it a single join
3) add caching layer
Given active 504s, rollback seems safest first.

Sean Gallagher: agree. is this endpoint used by anything other than beta console view? can we feature-flag it off for affected orgs?

Dylan Brooks: it’s behind `optimize_suggestions_beta` in Console, but the API is callable; still mostly beta accounts. disabling in console will reduce traffic but doesn’t solve the regression for anyone currently enabled.

Chloe Martin: I’d rather rollback service deploy and then we can ship hotfix w/ proper query. we can also temporarily force `include_details=false` server-side.

Sean Gallagher: ok. Felix — what’s the last good build tag?

Felix Schneider: last good was `optimize-suggestions@2026.02.21.3`. current is `2026.02.22.1`.

Sean Gallagher: rolling back to 02.21.3 now. (deploybot command)
`/deploy rollback optimize-suggestions --to 2026.02.21.3 --region us-east-1`

Aisha Bello: watching. also setting alert to page if 504s > 2%.

Allison Grant: while rollback runs, can you paste the query? want to capture for postmortem.

Chloe Martin: from logs (trimmed):
```
SELECT s.id, s.project_id, s.route_id, s.model, s.savings_usd_per_day, s.latency_p95_delta_ms, s.risk_level
FROM optimize_suggestions s
WHERE s.project_id = $1
ORDER BY s.savings_usd_per_day DESC
LIMIT $2 OFFSET $3;

-- then per-row:
SELECT * FROM optimize_suggestion_inputs WHERE suggestion_id = $1;
```
so yeah, N+1.

Felix Schneider: fix is to do either (a) join + aggregate inputs summary in one query, or (b) fetch inputs for all IDs with `WHERE suggestion_id = ANY($ids)` and map in memory.

Sean Gallagher: rollback status?

PagerDuty: [DEPLOY] Rollback initiated for optimize-suggestions in us-east-1.

Aisha Bello: p95 starting to trend down already as older pods take over. still above baseline but falling.

Dylan Brooks: also, UI: we can ship a quick patch to not request details until drawer open (it currently requests `include_details=true` to show confidence column). I can flip to “summary only” for the table.

Sean Gallagher: do it. reduces blast radius even after hotfix.

Allison Grant: for the backend fix, recommend approach (b) first (ANY(ids)) to avoid complicated SQL. just ensure payload size stays within perf budget.

Felix Schneider: agreed. also we should make `include_details` default false, and have strict contract in ADR.

Chloe Martin: +1. I’ll open a quick PR to enforce default false and add guardrails test.

Leila Farouk: fyi E2E tests don’t cover perf, but we can add a regression test verifying number of DB queries is bounded (e.g., <= 3) for list endpoint.

Sean Gallagher: love that. please add if quick.

PagerDuty: [DEPLOY] Rollback completed for optimize-suggestions in us-east-1.

Aisha Bello: metrics update: p95 now 360–410ms, p99 700ms. 504s cleared. :thumbsup:

Allison Grant: nice. let’s keep an eye for 30m.

Felix Schneider: I’ll prep hotfix PR: batch fetch inputs w/ `ANY($ids)` + make `include_details` opt-in.

Chloe Martin: I’ll file follow-up to add a DB index on `optimize_suggestion_inputs.suggestion_id` if not present (not sure it is), but batching will help either way.

Dylan Brooks: I’m updating Console query to request summary only; we can still show “confidence” column based on summary fields, and fetch detailed inputs on drawer open.

Sean Gallagher: action items:
- Felix: hotfix PR + backport
- Chloe: default include_details=false + add query-count test
- Dylan: console request change
- Aisha: keep alert sensitivity for next 24h

Aisha Bello: ack. I’ll annotate Grafana with rollback event and link this thread.

Allison Grant: please also add note to runbook: if p95 spikes + DB time increases, check for N+1 + serializer defaults.

Sean Gallagher: +1. closing out if stable. thanks all.

title: #eng-sre: 1771312009
preview:
Aisha Bello: pulling out action items from today’s perf-canary retro so we don’t lose them. pls reply in-thread if you’re an owner / can’t take it.

AIs (draft):
1) Tuning: min-samples + dampening for warning + page (reduce flaps) — owner: @Rafael Mendes
2) Runbook updates: add clearer “is it queueing vs runtime exec” decision tree + rollback triggers — owner: @Aisha Bello (with @Noah Patel for runtime bits)
3) Alert payload: fix dashboard links (region/model preselected) + add trace link + baseline artifact link — owner: @Allison Grant
4) Baseline drift handling: document/update policy + guardrails to avoid “baseline chasing prod noise” — owner: @Nadia Rahman
5) Cardinality follow-up: confirm label set + retention impact after adding queue/batch histos — owner: @Logan Wright
6) Paging hygiene: top-model page rules need explicit “sustained” definition + dedupe key in incident-bot — owner: @Rafael Mendes (loop in IncidentBot folks)

I’ll turn these into Linear if folks agree.

Rafael Mendes: :thumbsup: on (1) + (6). For (1) I want to propose:
- warnings: require 3 consecutive eval windows (10m) AND min 200 samples per (model,region,sku)
- paging: require 6 consecutive windows (60m total) AND min 800 samples
- additionally: if telemetry ingest lag >5m, suppress paging

Will post draft in ENG-4888 (new) and link here.

Sean Gallagher: +1. Let’s be careful on “min samples per region+sku” though, we don’t want to block small regions forever. maybe allow a fallback to “region only” aggregation if sample rate is low.

Noah Patel: for runbook (2): can we add a step early: “check if runtime build tag changed in last 2h” + “kernel selection id changed” (we have those tags now). That’s the fastest way we’ve been catching the real ones.

Aisha Bello: yep. I’ll add a top section:
- validate alert isn’t a canary outage
- check ingest delay
- check runtime build + kernel tag delta
then go into queueing vs exec.

Allison Grant: I can take (3). The current Grafana link is missing vars so it lands on default region and ppl have been clicking around.
Proposed alert payload fields to add:
- grafana_url (with var-model, var-region, var-sku)
- trace_search_url (tempo query w/ model+canary req_id)
- baseline_artifact_url (s3://… or whatever we decided)

Need to confirm canonical dashboard uid + var names w/ observability-pack.

Logan Wright: (5) ok. quick note: if we add batch_size histogram w/ too many buckets it’s going to blow up costs. We should settle on like 8 buckets and downsample older than 7d.
Also I want to lock the label set to:
model, model_version, region, sku, runtime_build, kernel_id, canary_profile
(no request_id, no prompt_bucket as a label — do it as a bucketed metric only).

Nadia Rahman: (4) yes. Baseline drift was the #1 confusion in the storm.
I’ll write up:
- baseline types: fixed (release) vs rolling (daily)
- when we allow baseline update (only if current is stable AND no open incidents)
- “shadow” candidate baseline so we can diff before switching

Also we need to decide: do we baseline per SKU always? I think yes for top models, optional for rest.

IncidentBot: (system) Reminder: incident dedupe key currently uses {alertname, model}. Region and sku are NOT included.

Rafael Mendes: ty bot :facepalm: that explains the spammy merges in the thread.
Action item add: dedupe key should be {alertname, model, region} at minimum. maybe sku too.

Sean Gallagher: add sku. we had the eu-west dedicated pool issue where only one sku family regressed.

Allison Grant: agree. also if we add “confidence” or “evaluation_id” we can keep updates in one thread.

Aisha Bello: ok updated list:
6) incident-bot dedupe key + sustained definition + suppress conditions (ingest lag) — @Rafael Mendes

I’m going to file Linear tickets now. will paste links.

Aisha Bello: created:
- ENG-4891 “Runbook updates: perf-canary regression alert” (me)
- ENG-4892 “Alert payload: fix Grafana/trace/baseline links” (Allison)
- ENG-4893 “Baseline drift policy + versioning doc” (Nadia)
- ENG-4894 “Metrics cardinality + retention follow-up for perf-canary” (Logan)

Rafael, lmk your ticket # once you make it.

Rafael Mendes: made:
- ENG-4895 “Alert tuning + anti-flap updates for perf-canary (warn/page)”
- ENG-4896 “Incident-bot dedupe + sustained regression semantics for perf-canary alerts”

Will have a first draft of threshold changes tomorrow EOD PST.

Noah Patel: re (2) can we also add a “quick mitigation” section:
- toggle canary QPS down (capacity budget)
- if only queueing regressed: check fleet saturation + autoscaler events
- if only exec regressed: consider runtime rollback vs kernel flag rollback

Aisha Bello: yep. I’ll add explicit “safe actions” vs “requires runtime lead approval”.

Sean Gallagher: please include who to page for kernel rollback approvals. last time we had confusion between runtime oncall vs kernels.

Aisha Bello: will add escalation matrix section + link to rotations.

Logan Wright: one more thing: can we add to alerts a “telemetry freshness” panel? if ingest delay is high we should mark the alert as “data delayed”. reduces noise.

Allison Grant: good call. I can add a small panel + also include current ingest lag in the payload (if we have it as a metric).

Nadia Rahman: FYI baseline store link: we should avoid raw s3 links in Slack (permissions). maybe point to the internal baseline viewer endpoint instead.

Allison Grant: yep, will do “baseline viewer” link. If missing perms, it will show a friendly error + request access.

Rafael Mendes: closing the loop: for the min-sample fallback Sean mentioned, I’ll implement:
- prefer model+region+sku
- if <min_samples for 2h straight, evaluate model+region (drop sku) but downgrade to warning-only (no paging)
Sound ok?

Sean Gallagher: sounds right.

Aisha Bello: :thumbsup: works for me. I’ll note that in ENG-4895 as a requirement.

Aisha Bello: last ask: owners pls update tickets w/ ETA + any cross-team deps by Fri so we can track in next runtime/SRE sync.

Allison Grant: ack, will update ENG-4892 today.

Logan Wright: same for ENG-4894.

Nadia Rahman: will update ENG-4893 once I confirm baseline viewer endpoint name w/ platform.

Rafael Mendes: will update ENG-4895/4896 after I get initial numbers from last 7d canary runs.

Sean Gallagher: thanks all. keeping this tight will save a lot of 3am pain.

3) Alert payload: fix dashboard links (region/model preselected) + add trace link + baseline artifact link — owner: @Allison Grant
4) Baseline drift handling: document/update policy + guardrails to avoid “baseline chasing prod noise” — owner: @Nadia Rahman
5) Cardinality follow-up: confirm label set + retention impact after adding queue/batch histos — owner: @Logan Wright
6) Paging hygiene: top-model page rules need explicit “sustained” definition + dedupe key in incident-bot — owner: @Rafael Mendes (loop in IncidentBot folks)

I’ll turn these into Linear if folks agree.

Rafael Mendes: :thumbsup: on (1) + (6). For (1) I want to propose:
- warnings: require 3 consecutive eval windows (10m) AND min 200 samples per (model,region,sku)
- paging: require 6 consecutive windows (60m total) AND min 800 samples
- additionally: if telemetry ingest lag >5m, suppress paging

Will post draft in ENG-4888 (new) and link here.

Sean Gallagher: +1. Let’s be careful on “min samples per region+sku” though, we don’t want to block small regions forever. maybe allow a fallback to “region only” aggregation if sample rate is low.

Noah Patel: for runbook (2): can we add a step early: “check if runtime build tag changed in last 2h” + “kernel selection id changed” (we have those tags now). That’s the fastest way we’ve been catching the real ones.

Aisha Bello: yep. I’ll add a top section:
- validate alert isn’t a canary outage
- check ingest delay
- check runtime build + kernel tag delta
then go into queueing vs exec.

Allison Grant: I can take (3). The current Grafana link is missing vars so it lands on default region and ppl have been clicking around.
Proposed alert payload fields to add:
- grafana_url (with var-model, var-region, var-sku)
- trace_search_url (tempo query w/ model+canary req_id)
- baseline_artifact_url (s3://… or whatever we decided)

Need to confirm canonical dashboard uid + var names w/ observability-pack.

Logan Wright: (5) ok. quick note: if we add batch_size histogram w/ too many buckets it’s going to blow up costs. We should settle on like 8 buckets and downsample older than 7d.
Also I want to lock the label set to:
model, model_version, region, sku, runtime_build, kernel_id, canary_profile
(no request_id, no prompt_bucket as a label — do it as a bucketed metric only).

Nadia Rahman: (4) yes. Baseline drift was the #1 confusion in the storm.
I’ll write up:
- baseline types: fixed (release) vs rolling (daily)
- when we allow baseline update (only if current is stable AND no open incidents)
- “shadow” candidate baseline so we can diff before switching

Also we need to decide: do we baseline per SKU always? I think yes for top models, optional for rest.

IncidentBot: (system) Reminder: incident dedupe key currently uses {alertname, model}. Region and sku are NOT included.

Rafael Mendes: ty bot :facepalm: that explains the spammy merges in the thread.
Action item add: dedupe key should be {alertname, model, region} at minimum. maybe sku too.

Sean Gallagher: add sku. we had the eu-west dedicated pool issue where only one sku family regressed.

Allison Grant: agree. also if we add “confidence” or “evaluation_id” we can keep updates in one thread.

Aisha Bello: ok updated list:
6) incident-bot dedupe key + sustained definition + suppress conditions (ingest lag) — @Rafael Mendes

I’m going to file Linear tickets now. will paste links.

Aisha Bello: created:
- ENG-4891 “Runbook updates: perf-canary regression alert” (me)
- ENG-4892 “Alert payload: fix Grafana/trace/baseline links” (Allison)
- ENG-4893 “Baseline drift policy + versioning doc” (Nadia)
- ENG-4894 “Metrics cardinality + retention follow-up for perf-canary” (Logan)

Rafael, lmk your ticket # once you make it.

Rafael Mendes: made:
- ENG-4895 “Alert tuning + anti-flap updates for perf-canary (warn/page)”
- ENG-4896 “Incident-bot dedupe + sustained regression semantics for perf-canary alerts”

Will have a first draft of threshold changes tomorrow EOD PST.

Noah Patel: re (2) can we also add a “quick mitigation” section:
- toggle canary QPS down (capacity budget)
- if only queueing regressed: check fleet saturation + autoscaler events
- if only exec regressed: consider runtime rollback vs kernel flag rollback

Aisha Bello: yep. I’ll add explicit “safe actions” vs “requires runtime lead approval”.

Sean Gallagher: please include who to page for kernel rollback approvals. last time we had confusion between runtime oncall vs kernels.

Aisha Bello: will add escalation matrix section + link to rotations.

Logan Wright: one more thing: can we add to alerts a “telemetry freshness” panel? if ingest delay is high we should mark the alert as “data delayed”. reduces noise.

Allison Grant: good call. I can add a small panel + also include current ingest lag in the payload (if we have it as a metric).

Nadia Rahman: FYI baseline store link: we should avoid raw s3 links in Slack (permissions). maybe point to the internal baseline viewer endpoint instead.

Allison Grant: yep, will do “baseline viewer” link. If missing perms, it will show a friendly error + request access.

Rafael Mendes: closing the loop: for the min-sample fallback Sean mentioned, I’ll implement:
- prefer model+region+sku
- if <min_samples for 2h straight, evaluate model+region (drop sku) but downgrade to warning-only (no paging)
Sound ok?

Sean Gallagher: sounds right.

Aisha Bello: :thumbsup: works for me. I’ll note that in ENG-4895 as a requirement.

Aisha Bello: last ask: owners pls update tickets w/ ETA + any cross-team deps by Fri so we can track in next runtime/SRE sync.

Allison Grant: ack, will update ENG-4892 today.

Logan Wright: same for ENG-4894.

Nadia Rahman: will update ENG-4893 once I confirm baseline viewer endpoint name w/ platform.

Rafael Mendes: will update ENG-4895/4896 after I get initial numbers from last 7d canary runs.

Sean Gallagher: thanks all. keeping this tight will save a lot of 3am pain.

title: #eng-sre: 1771169925
preview:
Aisha Bello: seeing a bunch of noise from the new auto-rollback alert rules in staging + a couple prod canaries. mostly `rollout_auto_action_triggered` and `rollout_slo_monitor_stale` flipping during the telemetry hiccup in eu-west. can we tune before we light up more services?

GrafanaBot: Alert triggered: rollout_slo_monitor_stale
Labels: service=rollout-controller region=eu-west tier=internal severity=page
Annotations: "SLO burn-rate endpoint returned stale data (age=198s) for 3/5 consecutive polls"

Sean Gallagher: yeah, paging for stale monitors during known telemetry issues is not what we want. we should be notifying (slack) not paging, and ideally suppress entirely when telemetry is degraded.

Rafael Mendes: +1. last night we had the metrics-ingest backlog, and everything lit up. oncall got 12 pages in 8 min. :/

Logan Wright: telemetry side: confirmed we had query lag from the burn-rate endpoint (slo-toolkit cache stampede + remote read latency). data was real but delayed. controller treated it as stale => 
- auto-actions suppressed (good)
- but alerts still fired + paged (bad)

Allison Grant: reminder from standards: missing/stale data should not page unless it blocks critical ops. for this feature, stale monitor should be "notify owner" not "page SRE" (at least MVP).

Aisha Bello: current rules are basically:
- page on any auto rollback
- page on stale monitors >2m
- notify on suppressed
…which sounded fine until the telemetry wobble.

Sean Gallagher: can we split severity based on environment + action? i.e.
- `ROLLBACK` => page (prod only)
- `PAUSE` => notify (prod), page (never)
- `MONITOR_STALE` => notify only, maybe ticket if sustained

Jordan Lee: also note: controller already marks decisions as `SUPPRESSED_TELEMETRY_OUTAGE` if staleness > policy max_age. so we can route on that reason.

Aisha Bello: yep, the metric has label `suppression_reason` now (from pr-28471). we can use it, but need to keep cardinality bounded.

Caleb Johnson: label set we shipped is bounded: decision={pause,rollback,suppress} + reason enum. should be safe. pls don’t add rollout_id to alerts.

Rafael Mendes: what about a global 
"telemetry outage" suppression? like, if we have a known incident, just inhibit all these alerts.

Logan Wright: we can expose a single gauge from telemetry pipeline health, something like `telemetry_pipeline_degraded{region}`. we already have internal signal in the ingest service, just not wired to prom in all envs.

Aisha Bello: short term patch: add Alertmanager inhibition rule when `telemetry_pipeline_degraded==1` OR when we have the `slo_toolkit_remote_read_errors` alert firing.

Sean Gallagher: +1. quick patch is fine. longer term we can integrate incident-based suppression but that’s harder.

Benji Okafor: policy engine side we also have a global flag we can set (`rollout.auto_actions.suppress_all=true`) but that only stops actions, not alerts. still useful, but doesn’t solve paging noise.

Jada Williams: controller behavior during telemetry outages is already fail-safe (suppresses rollback, emits audit). so we can safely lower alert severity without risking missing an actual rollback.

Aisha Bello: proposing changes (observability-pack patch):
1) `rollout_slo_monitor_stale` => severity=notify, add `for: 10m` (currently `for: 2m`)
2) `rollout_auto_action_triggered` => only page when `decision="rollback"` AND env=prod AND tier in {self-serve,dedicated}
3) add inhibition: inhibit both alerts when telemetry_pipeline_degraded=1 OR `slo_toolkit_remote_read_errors` firing

Allison Grant: +1 on `for: 10m` for stale. 2m is too tight given typical lag spikes.

Rafael Mendes: can we also rate-limit notifications? slack channel got spammy too.

Aisha Bello: we can group by service/region/route and set `group_interval` higher. currently it’s grouping by service only, which is ok, but `repeat_interval` is low.

Sean Gallagher: what’s your suggested alertmanager tweak?

Aisha Bello: something like:
```
route:
  receiver: slack-eng-sre
  group_by: ['alertname','service','region']
  group_wait: 30s
  group_interval: 10m
  repeat_interval: 4h

inhibit_rules:
- source_matchers:
  - alertname="telemetry_pipeline_degraded"
  target_matchers:
  - alertname=~"rollout_(slo_monitor_stale|auto_action_triggered)"
  equal: ['region']
```

Logan Wright: telemetry_pipeline_degraded alert exists in prod us-east/us-west, not in eu-west yet. we can also inhibit based on `slo_toolkit_remote_read_errors` which *did* fire in eu-west.

Caleb Johnson: +1 to grouping by region. we saw different behavior per region during the lag.

Jordan Lee: for the metric-based rule, we should be careful on label match. e.g., `decision="rollback"` is fine; `suppression_reason` match should be equality not regex to avoid future enums.

Jada Williams: also: our controller emits `decision="suppress" reason="stale_data"` and `reason="insufficient_samples"`. only stale_data should be inhibited by telemetry degraded. insufficient_samples is more config/traffic related.

Aisha Bello: good call. so inhibition is for `rollout_slo_monitor_stale` + `rollout_auto_action_triggered{decision="suppress",reason="stale_data"}`. leave other suppressions visible.

Rafael Mendes: can we downgrade the "suppress stale" alert to just a dashboard annotation? doesn’t need to be an alert at all.

Allison Grant: i’d keep a notify-level alert. monitor gaps/staleness was a big finding in the last simulation. but no paging.

Sean Gallagher: agree. notify-level is fine. paging should be only for confirmed rollbacks in prod.

Benji Okafor: do we need to adjust safe bounds? some services set `max_monitor_age_seconds=120`. during mild lag they’ll always suppress and we’ll alert forever.

Sean Gallagher: for now, set recommended default to 5m? (or 4m) and let services tighten if they really want. but still, alerts shouldn’t page.

Logan Wright: +1 to raising default max age. we can’t guarantee <2m in every region at peak.

Aisha Bello: ok action items:
- I’ll push a patch to observability-pack today adjusting severities + inhibition + grouping.
- Logan/Caleb: can you confirm which telemetry health signals are available in all regions and what label set we should rely on?

Logan Wright: yep, will reply in thread w/ current coverage and a short-term option.

Caleb Johnson: i can add `telemetry_pipeline_degraded` to eu-west prom scrape this afternoon. will ping when merged.

Rafael Mendes: and can we post in #eng-oncall that pages should quiet down once patched? last night was rough.

Sean Gallagher: do it. also please update runbook section: "if telemetry outage, expect suppressions; verify using telemetry health dashboard; do not override to force rollback." we don’t want folks flipping suppress flags blindly.

Aisha Bello: :thumbsup: will link the runbook section once merged.

GrafanaBot: Alert resolved: rollout_slo_monitor_stale
Labels: service=rollout-controller region=eu-west tier=internal severity=page
Annotations: "Burn-rate endpoint data age back to 42s"

title: #eng-sre: 1770932256
preview:
Sean Gallagher: We need to settle operational ownership for auto-rollback events. Who gets paged when an auto-rollback triggers? SRE vs service owner? Also how do we treat multi-tenant impact (Hosted pool vs Dedicated vs Private).

Aisha Bello: +1. I’m wiring alert rules now and need routing. Right now I have “auto_rollback_triggered” as notify-only in #eng-sre but not paging. Unsure if that’s enough.

Rafael Mendes: From oncall perspective: if rollback succeeded, it *already mitigated* the customer-facing issue. Paging on every trigger will be noisy.

Jada Williams: Agree. Controller will emit decision = pause/rollback/suppress. I’d propose paging only on “rollback_failed” or “monitor_missing/stale” or “suppressed_due_to_incident=false but still breached” type states.

Allison Grant: Also: multi-tenant vs single-tenant. A rollback on Hosted might indicate a systemic regression in shared fleet, not just one service. But most rollouts are service-scoped.

Chloe Martin: Practically: service teams own their rollouts (routes/models) but SRE owns the platform health and the rollout controller. If auto-rollback fires on a customer tier route, the service owner probably needs to look at what changed.

Sean Gallagher: Let’s define 2 tracks: (1) “feature is working” events (rollback succeeded) vs (2) “feature is failing / risk of SLA breach” events.

Aisha Bello: What about repeated triggers? If a rollout is thrashing (rollback, retry, rollback), could still burn error budget even if each rollback “succeeds”.

Ethan Park: Controller can include `rollout_id`, `service`, `route`, `model_version`, `region`, `customer_tier`. We *should not* include `customer_id` in alert labels (cardinality + privacy). But we can include tier and maybe “deployment_mode=hosted|dedicated|private”.

Allison Grant: +1 on not putting tenant ids into metrics labels. For Hosted, we can include `pool` or `capacity_tier` but even that can blow up. Keep it to small enumerations.

Rafael Mendes: Suggested paging policy:
- Notify Slack (no page) on `decision=rollback` or `decision=pause` when `execution_status=success`.
- Page SRE if `execution_status=failed` OR `monitor_state=missing|stale` OR `decision=suppress` due to telemetry outage (since that’s platform-ish).
- Page service owner if same service triggers >N auto actions in M minutes (means their rollout is unstable).

Sean Gallagher: I like that split, but for multi-tenant Hosted: if we see rollbacks across multiple services/regions within a short window, that’s a fleet issue -> page SRE.

Aisha Bello: I can do a composite alert: “>=3 unique services with auto-rollback within 10m in same region” (labels: region, deployment_mode). That would be paging to SRE.

Chloe Martin: For single service repeated rollbacks: service owner page makes sense *if they have an oncall*. Some smaller internal services don’t.

Connor O'Brien: Release-eng angle: if we page service owners, we need a consistent oncall mapping. Otherwise it’ll default to SRE anyway. Could we route by “owning_team” tag from service registry?

Ethan Park: We can look up `service -> owning_team -> pager` via the control plane registry. But that’s a v2. For MVP, might be simpler: page SRE only, and notify service owners in Slack.

Marissa Cole: Product POV: customers will ask “who saw this?” If a rollback triggers, someone should be accountable to investigate root cause. Doesn’t need to be an immediate page, but there must be a clear owner.

Rafael Mendes: Ownership != paging. We can set:
- Owner for follow-up: service team that initiated rollout.
- Pager for immediate response: SRE only when mitigation didn’t happen or risk persists.

Allison Grant: +1. Also consider Dedicated/Private: those are usually higher-touch. If Dedicated customer in region X rolls back, it might be just that customer’s reserved pool, not fleet. That should go to the account/service owner channel, not wake up global SRE unless it’s controller failure.

Aisha Bello: How do we detect “multi-tenant impact”? If `deployment_mode=hosted` and `customer_tier=self_serve`, it’s multi-tenant by definition. Dedicated/private should be single-tenant-ish.

Jada Williams: We can set a field in the audit event: `impact_scope=fleet|tenant` computed by controller based on deployment_mode + tier + route classification. That’s better than trying to infer in alerting.

Sean Gallagher: That’d be great. But for now, alert rules can approximate:
- hosted => potential fleet
- dedicated/private => tenant

Chloe Martin: One nuance: hosted enterprise tier (SLA) is still multi-tenant sometimes (shared pool but with priority). So “hosted == fleet” is mostly true.

Ethan Park: We can add `capacity_isolation=shared|dedicated_pool|customer_managed` to the event without high cardinality. That might help.

Aisha Bello: OK. Proposed alert routing v0:
1) Slack notify to #eng-sre + #eng-platform for every auto action event (pause/rollback/suppress) with links.
2) Page SRE on:
   - `execution_status=failed`
   - `monitor_state in {missing, stale}`
   - `suppression_reason=telemetry_outage` (or “monitor provider error”)
   - fleet pattern: >=3 unique services auto-rollback in 10m per region (hosted only)
3) Page service owner (later) on: same service >=3 auto actions in 30m (but only after we have registry -> pager mapping).

Rafael Mendes: Works for me. Also: no paging for “rollback succeeded” single event. That should be a ticket + follow-up.

Sean Gallagher: Add one more: if auto-rollback triggers for `customer_tier=private` and `region=onprem` (air-gapped) we might not have centralized visibility; still don’t page SRE unless execution failed. But we should notify the owning TAM / solutions channel. Maybe out of scope.

Marissa Cole: For MVP, I’m fine with SRE paging only on failure/missing monitor/fleet pattern. We can add “service owner follow-up required” banner in Console later.

incident-bot: Auto-rollback event notifications will be posted to Slack (non-paging) with rollout_id, service, region, decision, and dashboard links. (config pending)

Allison Grant: Please make sure the Slack message includes suppression status. It’s super confusing when it says “breach detected” but action is suppressed.

Jada Williams: Yup. Message format I’m aiming for:
- decision (pause/rollback/suppress)
- reason (latency_p99 burn, error_rate burn, monitor stale)
- policy (template name + version)
- scope (service/route/model/region/tier)
- link to audit event + Grafana

Aisha Bello: I’ll align alert labels with that. Also I’ll avoid including rollout_id as a label in the *alert rule* (cardinality), but it can be in the alert annotation/Slack text.

Sean Gallagher: Great. Action items:
- Aisha: implement routing rules above in observability-pack PR + note what’s paging vs notify.
- Jada/Ethan: add `capacity_isolation` (or similar) to audit event + Slack payload if easy.
- Rafael: update oncall runbook section “Who responds?” with this policy.

Rafael Mendes: Ack. I’ll add a decision table and make it explicit: “auto-rollback succeeded => notify + ticket; failed/missing monitor/fleet => page SRE.”

Connor O'Brien: Once we add service registry routing, loop me in. We can standardize on an `oncall_team` field in service metadata.

Sean Gallagher: :thumbsup: Let’s treat this as the default going into staging canary, and we’ll tune after first week of prod internal route enablement.

Allison Grant: Also: multi-tenant vs single-tenant. A rollback on Hosted might indicate a systemic regression in shared fleet, not just one service. But most rollouts are service-scoped.

Chloe Martin: Practically: service teams own their rollouts (routes/models) but SRE owns the platform health and the rollout controller. If auto-rollback fires on a customer tier route, the service owner probably needs to look at what changed.

Sean Gallagher: Let’s define 2 tracks: (1) “feature is working” events (rollback succeeded) vs (2) “feature is failing / risk of SLA breach” events.

Aisha Bello: What about repeated triggers? If a rollout is thrashing (rollback, retry, rollback), could still burn error budget even if each rollback “succeeds”.

Ethan Park: Controller can include `rollout_id`, `service`, `route`, `model_version`, `region`, `customer_tier`. We *should not* include `customer_id` in alert labels (cardinality + privacy). But we can include tier and maybe “deployment_mode=hosted|dedicated|private”.

Allison Grant: +1 on not putting tenant ids into metrics labels. For Hosted, we can include `pool` or `capacity_tier` but even that can blow up. Keep it to small enumerations.

Rafael Mendes: Suggested paging policy:
- Notify Slack (no page) on `decision=rollback` or `decision=pause` when `execution_status=success`.
- Page SRE if `execution_status=failed` OR `monitor_state=missing|stale` OR `decision=suppress` due to telemetry outage (since that’s platform-ish).
- Page service owner if same service triggers >N auto actions in M minutes (means their rollout is unstable).

Sean Gallagher: I like that split, but for multi-tenant Hosted: if we see rollbacks across multiple services/regions within a short window, that’s a fleet issue -> page SRE.

Aisha Bello: I can do a composite alert: “>=3 unique services with auto-rollback within 10m in same region” (labels: region, deployment_mode). That would be paging to SRE.

Chloe Martin: For single service repeated rollbacks: service owner page makes sense *if they have an oncall*. Some smaller internal services don’t.

Connor O'Brien: Release-eng angle: if we page service owners, we need a consistent oncall mapping. Otherwise it’ll default to SRE anyway. Could we route by “owning_team” tag from service registry?

Ethan Park: We can look up `service -> owning_team -> pager` via the control plane registry. But that’s a v2. For MVP, might be simpler: page SRE only, and notify service owners in Slack.

Marissa Cole: Product POV: customers will ask “who saw this?” If a rollback triggers, someone should be accountable to investigate root cause. Doesn’t need to be an immediate page, but there must be a clear owner.

Rafael Mendes: Ownership != paging. We can set:
- Owner for follow-up: service team that initiated rollout.
- Pager for immediate response: SRE only when mitigation didn’t happen or risk persists.

Allison Grant: +1. Also consider Dedicated/Private: those are usually higher-touch. If Dedicated customer in region X rolls back, it might be just that customer’s reserved pool, not fleet. That should go to the account/service owner channel, not wake up global SRE unless it’s controller failure.

Aisha Bello: How do we detect “multi-tenant impact”? If `deployment_mode=hosted` and `customer_tier=self_serve`, it’s multi-tenant by definition. Dedicated/private should be single-tenant-ish.

Jada Williams: We can set a field in the audit event: `impact_scope=fleet|tenant` computed by controller based on deployment_mode + tier + route classification. That’s better than trying to infer in alerting.

Sean Gallagher: That’d be great. But for now, alert rules can approximate:
- hosted => potential fleet
- dedicated/private => tenant

Chloe Martin: One nuance: hosted enterprise tier (SLA) is still multi-tenant sometimes (shared pool but with priority). So “hosted == fleet” is mostly true.

Ethan Park: We can add `capacity_isolation=shared|dedicated_pool|customer_managed` to the event without high cardinality. That might help.

Aisha Bello: OK. Proposed alert routing v0:
1) Slack notify to #eng-sre + #eng-platform for every auto action event (pause/rollback/suppress) with links.
2) Page SRE on:
   - `execution_status=failed`
   - `monitor_state in {missing, stale}`
   - `suppression_reason=telemetry_outage` (or “monitor provider error”)
   - fleet pattern: >=3 unique services auto-rollback in 10m per region (hosted only)
3) Page service owner (later) on: same service >=3 auto actions in 30m (but only after we have registry -> pager mapping).

Rafael Mendes: Works for me. Also: no paging for “rollback succeeded” single event. That should be a ticket + follow-up.

Sean Gallagher: Add one more: if auto-rollback triggers for `customer_tier=private` and `region=onprem` (air-gapped) we might not have centralized visibility; still don’t page SRE unless execution failed. But we should notify the owning TAM / solutions channel. Maybe out of scope.

Marissa Cole: For MVP, I’m fine with SRE paging only on failure/missing monitor/fleet pattern. We can add “service owner follow-up required” banner in Console later.

incident-bot: Auto-rollback event notifications will be posted to Slack (non-paging) with rollout_id, service, region, decision, and dashboard links. (config pending)

Allison Grant: Please make sure the Slack message includes suppression status. It’s super confusing when it says “breach detected” but action is suppressed.

Jada Williams: Yup. Message format I’m aiming for:
- decision (pause/rollback/suppress)
- reason (latency_p99 burn, error_rate burn, monitor stale)
- policy (template name + version)
- scope (service/route/model/region/tier)
- link to audit event + Grafana

Aisha Bello: I’ll align alert labels with that. Also I’ll avoid including rollout_id as a label in the *alert rule* (cardinality), but it can be in the alert annotation/Slack text.

Sean Gallagher: Great. Action items:
- Aisha: implement routing rules above in observability-pack PR + note what’s paging vs notify.
- Jada/Ethan: add `capacity_isolation` (or similar) to audit event + Slack payload if easy.
- Rafael: update oncall runbook section “Who responds?” with this policy.

Rafael Mendes: Ack. I’ll add a decision table and make it explicit: “auto-rollback succeeded => notify + ticket; failed/missing monitor/fleet => page SRE.”

Connor O'Brien: Once we add service registry routing, loop me in. We can standardize on an `oncall_team` field in service metadata.

Sean Gallagher: :thumbsup: Let’s treat this as the default going into staging canary, and we’ll tune after first week of prod internal route enablement.

title: #eng-sre: 1770890003
preview:
Aisha Bello: Heads up: doing a short oncall training session for perf-canary alerts (warn/page). Target audience: SRE oncall + runtime secondary. 

Proposed time: Thu 11:00–11:45 PT. Agenda:
1) What the canary is / what it is NOT
2) How to validate a warning vs a page
3) Quick attribution: queueing vs runtime vs fleet
4) Mitigation + rollback + disable switch
5) Q&A

Pls reply with :thumbsup: if you can make it, and any questions you want covered.

Sean Gallagher: :thumbsup: works. Can we make sure we cover “what do I do if dashboards are behind” / telemetry lag? We hit that last week.

Rafael Mendes: :thumbsup: +1. Also want clarity on paging hygiene: when do we wake runtime vs handle as SRE with a mitigation.

Allison Grant: :thumbsup: I can join. Suggest we also explicitly cover dedupe/anti-flap so folks don’t panic when they see multiple alerts for same model.

Noah Patel: :thumbsup: I’ll attend and can do the 5-min “how regression evaluator decides” bit. We should align on: minimum samples, dampening window, and how to interpret confidence field in the alert payload.

Sofia Ivanova: :thumbsup: I can cover scheduler signals (queueing time, batch size distribution) and what “looks like capacity” vs “looks like kernel”.

Logan Wright: :thumbsup: I’ll join for telemetry questions. If useful I can share current ingestion delay SLO + where to check pipeline health.

Nadia Rahman: :thumbsup: joining. Happy to answer baseline store questions (rolling vs fixed, and why we don’t update baselines automatically during incidents).

Omar El-Sayed: can make it. If we have 2 mins, I can show where runtime build id + kernel tag show up in metrics/traces.

Aisha Bello: Great. I’ll send a calendar + Zoom in a sec.

Aisha Bello: Invite sent: “Perf-canary oncall training” Thu 11:00–11:45 PT. Notes doc here (please add Qs): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/x/perf-canary-oncall-training

Rafael Mendes: added Qs in doc. One more here: what’s the decision rule for “warn in #eng-runtime” vs “page oncall”? Is it strictly top models list?

Aisha Bello: Yep mostly. Paging is limited to “top models” + sustained regression condition. We’ll walk through the policy on the call. If it’s not a top model, we should not page unless it’s cascading / fleet-wide symptoms.

Sean Gallagher: can we include one worked example from a real alert thread? (what the bot posted, what to click, what query to run)

Aisha Bello: +1. I’ll use the last sustained regression example (sanitized) and the false-positive storm example for “what NOT to do.”

Allison Grant: For the worked example: please show the alert payload fields and what’s actionable. Folks get stuck on “observed vs baseline vs threshold” and whether that’s already adjusted for region/SKU.

Noah Patel: Good call. Current payload:
- model + model_version
- region
- sku
- metric (p95_e2e_ms, tokens_per_sec)
- observed value + baseline value
- percent_change
- min_samples + samples_observed
- confidence (roughly: fraction of window exceeding threshold)

We should emphasize region+sku can be “all” depending on config; don’t assume.

Logan Wright: Also: please remind folks we intentionally keep label set small. If you need per-node drilldown, use tracing links, not metrics. (We got a bunch of “where’s the host id” questions.)

Sofia Ivanova: Q: should oncall look at queueing time first? IMO yes, because capacity/queueing causes lots of tail regressions.

Aisha Bello: Agree. Proposed triage order in runbook:
1) confirm it’s real (dashboards + sample size + check ingestion lag)
2) check queueing + batch size
3) check runtime build/kernel tag changes
4) check fleet changes

Rafael Mendes: what’s the “ingestion lag” check specifically? I always forget where to look.

Logan Wright: There’s a Grafana panel “telemetry pipeline lag” in the canary dashboard folder. Also quick check: if canary service logs show request timestamps are current but metrics query looks stale, it’s ingestion.

Aisha Bello: I’ll put the direct link in the doc.

Connor O'Brien: jumping in—can you also cover how rollout gates will consume this? (Even if it’s just: we don’t auto-rollback yet, oncall should coordinate with releases.)

Aisha Bello: Yep. We’ll include a slide: “What happens when canary triggers during a rollout” (pause expansion, notify #eng-releases, optional rollback).

Incident Bot: (FYI) New training event detected: Perf-canary oncall training (Thu 11:00 PT). Reminder will be posted 10 min before start.

--- Thu 10:51 PT ---

Incident Bot: Reminder: Perf-canary oncall training starts in 10 minutes. Zoom link from calendar invite.

--- After the session ---

Aisha Bello: Thanks all for joining. Dumping Q&A + decisions here for folks who missed.

Q: If I get a WARNING in #eng-runtime, do I need to open an incident?
A (Rafael/Sean): No. Treat as signal for runtime team; oncall only opens incident if (a) paging alert fires, or (b) multiple models/regions degrade, or (c) customer impact tickets start.

Q: If dashboards look “flat” / behind, how do I verify the canary is actually running?
A (Logan): Check canary service logs in Loki for recent requests; check “telemetry pipeline lag” panel. If lag > ~5–10 min, don’t treat deltas as real-time. Use logs/traces and ping #eng-platform.

Q: What’s the fastest way to tell capacity/queueing vs runtime regression?
A (Sofia): Look at queueing_time_p95 and batch_size distribution. If queueing jumps + batch size collapses, likely capacity/scheduler pressure. If queueing stable but runtime_only latency rises, likely kernel/runtime.

Q: The alert payload says “confidence=0.62”. What does that mean?
A (Noah): Fraction of evaluation windows in last N minutes exceeding threshold. <0.5 often flappy; we require sustained confidence for paging. Still sanity check samples_observed.

Q: Are baselines rolling? Can they drift and cause storms?
A (Nadia): Baselines are versioned per model_version/runtime_build/sku (and sometimes region). They do not auto-update in prod during an incident. We update baselines intentionally after validation runs. Drift is handled via thresholds + min samples + dampening.

Q: When do we use the disable switch for canary?
A (Sean): If canary overhead is suspected (GPU pressure) OR it’s generating alert storms due to known config bug. Disable is safe; it should not impact customer inference. Notify #eng-runtime + #eng-platform when disabling.

Action items:
- Aisha: add direct links to lag dashboard + runbook section “telemetry lag”
- Noah: update runbook with “confidence” definition + sample size callout
- Sofia: add screenshots of queueing vs runtime-only example panels
- Logan: document where to check ingestion delay SLO + who to page

Rafael Mendes: Super helpful. One ask: can we add a one-liner in the paging alert that says “Start with queueing panel” w/ link? People jump straight to runtime graphs.

Aisha Bello: Yep, I’ll tweak the alert annotation text and include the queueing panel deep link.

Allison Grant: Also please add “don’t compare across regions unless sku matches” in the runbook. We’ve seen folks panic when eu-west is naturally different.

Noah Patel: +1. We’re adding guardrails so baseline keys include sku explicitly; but runbook note is good.

Logan Wright: I added a note in the doc: pipeline lag panel is here: https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/perf-canary/telemetry-lag (internal)

Sean Gallagher: Thanks. For folks who missed: recording is in the calendar event notes (internal drive). Also reminder: if you page runtime, drop the alert thread link + model + region + runtime build id.

Aisha Bello: Closing the loop: notes doc updated with Q&A + links. Ping me if you want a repeat session for your rotation.

title: #eng-sre: 1770619940
preview:
Aisha Bello: pulling folks in here re: dashboards + alert routing for auto-rollback. need to decide what *pages* vs what’s slack-only, and how we tag rollout ids so oncall can investigate quickly (without blowing up cardinality). anyone have strong opinions before I update alert rules in observability-pack?

Sean Gallagher: yup. biggest thing: we can’t page every time a rollback triggers, otherwise we’ll get paged during normal safe operation. paging should be for “safety rail failed” or “high-impact condition”.

Allison Grant: +1. also rollout_id as a metric label is going to be a no from standards. it’ll explode. should be annotation / dashboard variable / log correlation.

Rafael Mendes: agreed. from oncall perspective, I want:
- a single dashboard where I can answer “what rollout changed?”
- one link in the alert that takes me to rollout timeline + decision explanation
- and a clear “is this paging-worthy or just FYI?” policy

Logan Wright: on tagging: we can do correlation_id consistently across controller logs + audit event stream. then alert can include correlation_id + rollout_id in annotations (labels stay low-card: service/route/region/tier).

Caleb Johnson: re metrics: if we add rollout_id as a label on `rollout_slo_eval_*` we’ll get murdered by prometheus. recommend:
- keep labels: `service`, `route`, `region`, `customer_tier`, `action` (pause/rollback/suppress), maybe `model_family`
- rollout_id goes in event/audit log + exemplar-like link in annotation (`runbook_url`, `console_url`)

Aisha Bello: ok so proposal for alert classes:
1) “Auto-rollback triggered” => slack notify (no page)
2) “Auto-rollback triggered N times in M minutes for same service/route/region” => page? (might indicate systemic regression)
3) “Rollback execution failed” => page (safety rail broke)
4) “Monitor missing/stale for rollout scope” => slack notify, page only if production + persists > X?
5) “Rollback suppressed due to admin override / incident mode” => slack notify (to avoid surprises)
thoughts?

Sean Gallagher: close. tweak:
- (1) slack notify to #eng-oncall + service channel (if we can route by owning team), but no PD
- (3) definitely page; sev2 if prod, sev3 if staging
- (4) missing/stale should not page immediately, but if auto-rollback enabled for prod service and we have *no valid signal*, that’s risk. i’d page only when it blocks a rollout AND service is in “auto actions required” group (dedicated/private tiers)

Connor O'Brien: from release side: please don’t page on “auto-rollback triggered” during staged enablement. we’re expecting some early tune-ups. paging only on execution failure seems right.

Allison Grant: we should be explicit in rule descriptions: “auto-rollback fired” is an expected mechanism. paging on it trains the org to ignore pages.

Jordan Lee: question: do we need separate alerts for pause vs rollback? pause could be noisier (fast-burn triggers) but still useful.

Rafael Mendes: oncall triage: pause = “something’s off, rollout stopped” and we need human to look. rollback = “system took corrective action”. I’d actually want *pause* to be higher priority than rollback in terms of humans paying attention.

Sean Gallagher: good point. but still not a page by default. maybe:
- pause => slack notify with “rollout blocked” language
- rollback => slack notify with “mitigation executed” language
- execution failure => page
- repeated pauses for same service in a short window => page (it’s effectively a stuck deploy train)

Aisha Bello: ok, let’s define paging conditions as:
A) `rollout_auto_action_executor_errors_total > 0` over 5m (any env=prod) => page
B) `rollout_auto_action_decisions_total{action="pause"}` rate high for same (service, route, region) for 15m => page? maybe threshold = >=3 pauses/15m
C) “monitor stale/missing” only pages if rollout is in progressing state + auto actions enabled + stale persists > 30m (prod) ?

Caleb Johnson: +1 but keep labels minimal. for B we can do it without rollout_id label, just aggregate by service/route/region/tier.

Allison Grant: also add a “noise guard”: only alert if `requests >= min_sample` is satisfied or decision outcome indicates sample gating already passed. else we’ll get alert spam during low traffic.

Logan Wright: we can add metric dimension `gated="true|false"` for eval outcomes? or better: `result` already includes “insufficient_sample”. alerts should ignore that.

Aisha Bello: yep. I’ll filter alerts to `decision in (pause, rollback)` and exclude `reason="insufficient_sample"` / `reason="stale"` for the high priority ones.

Jordan Lee: for tagging rollout_id: could we include rollout_id as an annotation (templated from the event stream) rather than as a label? i.e. alert fires on aggregate metric, but message includes “latest rollout_id seen” from a query?

Caleb Johnson: grafana alerting can’t reliably “join” a second query into annotations (it’s hacky and sometimes empty). best is to link to dashboard panel that can show the last few events with rollout_id from logs/event stream.

Rafael Mendes: +1000. please just give me a link that works. i don’t want to fight grafana templating at 3am.

Aisha Bello: ok, message template includes:
- `service`, `route`, `region`, `tier`
- action + reason
- link 1: "Rollout auto-actions dashboard" (filtered by those labels)
- link 2: "Console rollout timeline" (generic search page filtered by service/route/region + time range)
- link 3: "Runbook: Auto-rollback on SLO breach" (once Kira posts it)

Sean Gallagher: also include “suppression state” if applicable. we’ve had confusion when alerts fire but actions are suppressed.

Allison Grant: suppression should be in metric labels as a bounded enum (e.g. `suppressed_reason="admin_override|incident_mode|policy|unknown"`). that’s safe.

Logan Wright: we’re emitting audit events with suppression reason already (per ADR-0128 draft). i’ll make sure controller exports a matching metric label too.

Grafana Alerting: (bot) New alert rule created by @aisha.bello: `RolloutAutoActionExecutorFailure` (folder: Rollouts / Auto Actions) [Status: Pending review]

Connor O'Brien: routing: can we keep all these alerts non-paging by default in staging? release train will run a bunch of tests and i don’t want PD noise.

Aisha Bello: yes. environment label exists. plan:
- staging: slack only to #eng-platform-testing
- prod: page only for executor failure + repeated pauses; everything else slack to #eng-oncall

Rafael Mendes: for #eng-oncall notifications, can we add “owner” field or mention? if it’s always @sre-oncall it’s okay, but ideally service owner gets pinged.

Sean Gallagher: owner mapping is messy. maybe phase 2. for now, include `service` and a note “loop in service owner” and we’ll build a mapping later.

Allison Grant: yes, do not hardcode mentions without an ownership source-of-truth. we’ve been burned.

Jordan Lee: do we need a separate alert for “rollback suppressed” so folks don’t assume mitigation happened?

Aisha Bello: I’m planning a slack notify for suppressed decisions, but not page. message would be: “Auto-action suppressed (reason=incident_mode/admin_override). rollout continues at current step.”

Sean Gallagher: perfect. that’s exactly when humans should pay attention.

Caleb Johnson: one more: alert on “monitor missing” should be extremely clear about *what* is missing. include monitor_id? if that’s stable and not high-card.

Allison Grant: monitor_id is fine if it’s from a bounded set per service/route/region (should be), but if it’s generated per rollout scope it could still be high. prefer `monitor_kind` + link to monitor definition.

Logan Wright: our slo-toolkit burn-rate endpoint uses stable SLO names; monitor id is consistent per route/model version though. model version might create cardinality. safest: don’t label monitor_id, but include it in the audit event and link.

Rafael Mendes: works. as long as the dashboard has a table “recent auto-actions” with rollout_id + monitor link.

Aisha Bello: ok decisions:
- No rollout_id as metric label
- Alerts:
  - Page: rollback/pause executor failure (prod), repeated pauses (prod)
  - Notify-only: rollback triggered, pause triggered, suppressed, monitor stale/missing
- Messages include links to dashboard + console search view; rollout_id shown in dashboard via event stream / logs
I’ll update PR-913 accordingly and drop a review request.

incident-bot: (bot) Reminder: If you change paging rules, update oncall docs + notify #eng-oncall-leads.

Sean Gallagher: please add a short section to the runbook: “Alert meaning and expected response” (what we do for notify-only vs page). Rafael and I can review.

Rafael Mendes: +1. and add a note about “if you see repeated pauses, treat as deploy train blocked; escalate to service owner + releases”.

Aisha Bello: will do. I’ll also add a dashboard panel “top services by auto-action count (24h)” to spot noisy configs.

Allison Grant: and add label cardinality note in the observability standard doc when you land it. specifically call out rollout_id.

Aisha Bello: ack. thanks all.
text:
Aisha Bello: pulling folks in here re: dashboards + alert routing for auto-rollback. need to decide what *pages* vs what’s slack-only, and how we tag rollout ids so oncall can investigate quickly (without blowing up cardinality). anyone have strong opinions before I update alert rules in observability-pack?

Sean Gallagher: yup. biggest thing: we can’t page every time a rollback triggers, otherwise we’ll get paged during normal safe operation. paging should be for “safety rail failed” or “high-impact condition”.

Allison Grant: +1. also rollout_id as a metric label is going to be a no from standards. it’ll explode. should be annotation / dashboard variable / log correlation.

Rafael Mendes: agreed. from oncall perspective, I want:
- a single dashboard where I can answer “what rollout changed?”
- one link in the alert that takes me to rollout timeline + decision explanation
- and a clear “is this paging-worthy or just FYI?” policy

Logan Wright: on tagging: we can do correlation_id consistently across controller logs + audit event stream. then alert can include correlation_id + rollout_id in annotations (labels stay low-card: service/route/region/tier).

Caleb Johnson: re metrics: if we add rollout_id as a label on `rollout_slo_eval_*` we’ll get murdered by prometheus. recommend:
- keep labels: `service`, `route`, `region`, `customer_tier`, `action` (pause/rollback/suppress), maybe `model_family`
- rollout_id goes in event/audit log + exemplar-like link in annotation (`runbook_url`, `console_url`)

Aisha Bello: ok so proposal for alert classes:
1) “Auto-rollback triggered” => slack notify (no page)
2) “Auto-rollback triggered N times in M minutes for same service/route/region” => page? (might indicate systemic regression)
3) “Rollback execution failed” => page (safety rail broke)
4) “Monitor missing/stale for rollout scope” => slack notify, page only if production + persists > X?
5) “Rollback suppressed due to admin override / incident mode” => slack notify (to avoid surprises)
thoughts?

Sean Gallagher: close. tweak:
- (1) slack notify to #eng-oncall + service channel (if we can route by owning team), but no PD
- (3) definitely page; sev2 if prod, sev3 if staging
- (4) missing/stale should not page immediately, but if auto-rollback enabled for prod service and we have *no valid signal*, that’s risk. i’d page only when it blocks a rollout AND service is in “auto actions required” group (dedicated/private tiers)

Connor O'Brien: from release side: please don’t page on “auto-rollback triggered” during staged enablement. we’re expecting some early tune-ups. paging only on execution failure seems right.

Allison Grant: we should be explicit in rule descriptions: “auto-rollback fired” is an expected mechanism. paging on it trains the org to ignore pages.

Jordan Lee: question: do we need separate alerts for pause vs rollback? pause could be noisier (fast-burn triggers) but still useful.

Rafael Mendes: oncall triage: pause = “something’s off, rollout stopped” and we need human to look. rollback = “system took corrective action”. I’d actually want *pause* to be higher priority than rollback in terms of humans paying attention.

Sean Gallagher: good point. but still not a page by default. maybe:
- pause => slack notify with “rollout blocked” language
- rollback => slack notify with “mitigation executed” language
- execution failure => page
- repeated pauses for same service in a short window => page (it’s effectively a stuck deploy train)

Aisha Bello: ok, let’s define paging conditions as:
A) `rollout_auto_action_executor_errors_total > 0` over 5m (any env=prod) => page
B) `rollout_auto_action_decisions_total{action="pause"}` rate high for same (service, route, region) for 15m => page? maybe threshold = >=3 pauses/15m
C) “monitor stale/missing” only pages if rollout is in progressing state + auto actions enabled + stale persists > 30m (prod) ?

Caleb Johnson: +1 but keep labels minimal. for B we can do it without rollout_id label, just aggregate by service/route/region/tier.

Allison Grant: also add a “noise guard”: only alert if `requests >= min_sample` is satisfied or decision outcome indicates sample gating already passed. else we’ll get alert spam during low traffic.

Logan Wright: we can add metric dimension `gated="true|false"` for eval outcomes? or better: `result` already includes “insufficient_sample”. alerts should ignore that.

Aisha Bello: yep. I’ll filter alerts to `decision in (pause, rollback)` and exclude `reason="insufficient_sample"` / `reason="stale"` for the high priority ones.

Jordan Lee: for tagging rollout_id: could we include rollout_id as an annotation (templated from the event stream) rather than as a label? i.e. alert fires on aggregate metric, but message includes “latest rollout_id seen” from a query?

Caleb Johnson: grafana alerting can’t reliably “join” a second query into annotations (it’s hacky and sometimes empty). best is to link to dashboard panel that can show the last few events with rollout_id from logs/event stream.

Rafael Mendes: +1000. please just give me a link that works. i don’t want to fight grafana templating at 3am.

Aisha Bello: ok, message template includes:
- `service`, `route`, `region`, `tier`
- action + reason
- link 1: "Rollout auto-actions dashboard" (filtered by those labels)
- link 2: "Console rollout timeline" (generic search page filtered by service/route/region + time range)
- link 3: "Runbook: Auto-rollback on SLO breach" (once Kira posts it)

Sean Gallagher: also include “suppression state” if applicable. we’ve had confusion when alerts fire but actions are suppressed.

Allison Grant: suppression should be in metric labels as a bounded enum (e.g. `suppressed_reason="admin_override|incident_mode|policy|unknown"`). that’s safe.

Logan Wright: we’re emitting audit events with suppression reason already (per ADR-0128 draft). i’ll make sure controller exports a matching metric label too.

Grafana Alerting: (bot) New alert rule created by @aisha.bello: `RolloutAutoActionExecutorFailure` (folder: Rollouts / Auto Actions) [Status: Pending review]

Connor O'Brien: routing: can we keep all these alerts non-paging by default in staging? release train will run a bunch of tests and i don’t want PD noise.

Aisha Bello: yes. environment label exists. plan:
- staging: slack only to #eng-platform-testing
- prod: page only for executor failure + repeated pauses; everything else slack to #eng-oncall

Rafael Mendes: for #eng-oncall notifications, can we add “owner” field or mention? if it’s always @sre-oncall it’s okay, but ideally service owner gets pinged.

Sean Gallagher: owner mapping is messy. maybe phase 2. for now, include `service` and a note “loop in service owner” and we’ll build a mapping later.

Allison Grant: yes, do not hardcode mentions without an ownership source-of-truth. we’ve been burned.

Jordan Lee: do we need a separate alert for “rollback suppressed” so folks don’t assume mitigation happened?

Aisha Bello: I’m planning a slack notify for suppressed decisions, but not page. message would be: “Auto-action suppressed (reason=incident_mode/admin_override). rollout continues at current step.”

Sean Gallagher: perfect. that’s exactly when humans should pay attention.

Caleb Johnson: one more: alert on “monitor missing” should be extremely clear about *what* is missing. include monitor_id? if that’s stable and not high-card.

Allison Grant: monitor_id is fine if it’s from a bounded set per service/route/region (should be), but if it’s generated per rollout scope it could still be high. prefer `monitor_kind` + link to monitor definition.

Logan Wright: our slo-toolkit burn-rate endpoint uses stable SLO names; monitor id is consistent per route/model version though. model version might create cardinality. safest: don’t label monitor_id, but include it in the audit event and link.

Rafael Mendes: works. as long as the dashboard has a table “recent auto-actions” with rollout_id + monitor link.

Aisha Bello: ok decisions:
- No rollout_id as metric label
- Alerts:
  - Page: rollback/pause executor failure (prod), repeated pauses (prod)
  - Notify-only: rollback triggered, pause triggered, suppressed, monitor stale/missing
- Messages include links to dashboard + console search view; rollout_id shown in dashboard via event stream / logs
I’ll update PR-913 accordingly and drop a review request.

incident-bot: (bot) Reminder: If you change paging rules, update oncall docs + notify #eng-oncall-leads.

Sean Gallagher: please add a short section to the runbook: “Alert meaning and expected response” (what we do for notify-only vs page). Rafael and I can review.

Rafael Mendes: +1. and add a note about “if you see repeated pauses, treat as deploy train blocked; escalate to service owner + releases”.

Aisha Bello: will do. I’ll also add a dashboard panel “top services by auto-action count (24h)” to spot noisy configs.

Allison Grant: and add label cardinality note in the observability standard doc when you land it. specifically call out rollout_id.

Aisha Bello: ack. thanks all.

title: #eng-sre: 1770508842
preview:
Aisha Bello: for the mixed-workload scheduler canary, we need to lock down: (1) dashboard panels we’ll watch and (2) concrete rollback triggers. want this agreed before Rafael/Connor schedule the ramp.

Sean Gallagher: +1. also please keep it simple: if p95 improves but we blow up p99 or error budget, we roll back. no heroics.

Rafael Mendes: timeline wise, we’re targeting us-east hosted canary + one Dedicated pool. I need the exact alert thresholds + where they route (#eng-sre / pager?)

Julian Moore: instrumentation PR is in review (per-class queue depth, time-in-queue, starvation counter, dispatch share). naming proposal (prometheus):
- `runtime_scheduler_queue_depth{class="short|long",pool,model}`
- `runtime_scheduler_time_in_queue_ms_bucket{class,...}` (hist)
- `runtime_scheduler_starvation_events_total{class,...}`
- `runtime_scheduler_dispatch_share{class,...}` (gauge per minute)
- `runtime_scheduler_token_budget_overrun_total{class,...}`
Also exporting TTFT + e2e latency split by class via existing request metrics labels.

Allison Grant: ok. for dashboards, I’d like 3 rows:
1) tail latency + TTFT (overall + per class)
2) scheduler health (queue depth, time-in-queue, dispatch share)
3) guardrails (starvation, budget overruns, CPU fallback / saturation)

Lena Fischer: confirm: short/long classes are internal only; end users don’t see. canary will set `short_bias_weight=0.7` and `aging_half_life_s=15` unless SRE wants different.

Aisha Bello: for canary we should alert on *changes* vs baseline, not absolute only. but we can still do some absolute cutoffs.

Allison Grant: agreed. baseline window: previous 7d same hour? might be too fancy. simplest: compare to control pool running old scheduler.

Marcus Reed: we can run A/B pools: `pool=api-use1-canary` vs `pool=api-use1-control`. label already exists.

Aisha Bello: perfect. rollback triggers proposal (hosted canary):
- `p95` end-to-end latency (chat/generate) in canary > control by **+20%** for **>= 15m**
- `p99` end-to-end latency > control by **+30%** for **>= 10m**
- TTFT p95 > control by **+25%** for **>= 15m**
- 5xx rate > control by **+0.2% absolute** for **>= 10m** (or > 0.5% absolute if control is quiet)
- starvation counter: `rate(runtime_scheduler_starvation_events_total[5m]) > 0` for **>= 5m** (any class)
- long queue waiting time: `p95(time_in_queue_ms{class="long"}) > 5000ms` for **>= 10m** (signals we’re not making progress)

Sean Gallagher: add one for “queue depth runaway” as early warning. we’ve been burned there.

Allison Grant: +1. maybe: `runtime_scheduler_queue_depth{class="long"} > 2x` control for 10m OR absolute > N (depending on qps).

Julian Moore: queue depth is tricky because qps is bursty. I can add a panel for `queue_depth / dispatch_rate` (approx queueing delay). but as an alert, time-in-queue is more stable.

Aisha Bello: ok, let’s do:
- if `p95(time_in_queue_ms)` overall > control by +50% for 15m -> page SRE (not auto rollback)
- hard rollback is the tail latency + starvation + error rate conditions above.

Rafael Mendes: who is actually flipping the flag during ramp? if rollback triggers, do we auto-disable or manual?

Connor O'Brien: releases can do manual rollback if we have clear conditions + owner on point. auto-disable is risky unless we’re 100% sure.

Sean Gallagher: manual rollback but with paging. if it pages, expectation is rollback within 10 min unless there’s a known external incident.

Allison Grant: routing plan:
- warnings -> #eng-sre + #eng-runtime
- pages -> SRE oncall (pager) + #incidents
For pages we should include the control delta and direct Grafana link.

Logan Wright: can we ensure metric labels are low-cardinality? pool+model+class is fine. don’t add request route.

Julian Moore: yep, no route labels. model is capped set. pool is limited.

Allison Grant: I’ll draft the dashboard spec + alert rules in Grafana. naming: “Runtime Scheduler (Mixed Workload) - Class Metrics”. link will go into the rollout runbook.

Aisha Bello: also want a panel that shows `dispatch_share{class}` over time. if short share pins at ~1.0 for long periods, it’ll look “good” on short p95 but long queue will die.

Lena Fischer: the policy has aging; in theory long share should recover. but yes, panel makes sense.

Sean Gallagher: final question: what’s acceptable throughput loss? PM asked.

Aisha Bello: for canary gate: we won’t roll back for <= 5% throughput drop if tail latency improves; but if throughput drops > 10% sustained, treat as regression (warn), > 15% (rollback). can compute via tokens/s metric.

Julian Moore: tokens/s per pool is already in `runtime_tokens_generated_total` and `runtime_tokens_prompt_total` -> derive. I’ll add a note in metric docs.

Grafana Bot: Alert rule created: "Scheduler starvation events (any class)" (severity=page) -> SRE Oncall. https://grafana.redwoodinference.net/alerting/rules/runtime-scheduler-starvation

Allison Grant: nice. I’ll add the rest today. draft dashboard link (WIP): https://grafana.redwoodinference.net/d/rt-sched-class/runtime-scheduler-mixed-workload

Rafael Mendes: can we pin the final thresholds in a single message so I can copy into canary plan?

Aisha Bello: ✅ final rollback triggers (manual rollback, page SRE oncall):
1) p95 e2e latency canary > control +20% for 15m
2) p99 e2e latency canary > control +30% for 10m
3) TTFT p95 canary > control +25% for 15m
4) 5xx rate canary > control +0.2% abs for 10m (or >0.5% abs regardless)
5) starvation: `rate(starvation_events_total[5m]) > 0` for 5m
6) long class p95 time-in-queue > 5000ms for 10m
7) throughput drop > 15% vs control for 15m (warn at 10%)
Warnings (no immediate rollback): queue time p95 > control +50% for 15m; budget overrun rate spikes.

Sean Gallagher: :thumbsup: good. please make sure we have the incident template ready if it pages.

Connor O'Brien: I’ll update the release checklist + make sure #eng-releases has the links. we’ll schedule ramp after Allison publishes the dashboard as non-WIP.

Allison Grant: will do. I’ll also add annotations for flag flips so the graphs tell the story.

Lena Fischer: thanks all. once dashboards are live, I’ll run a quick dry-run in staging to make sure labels show up correctly.

Aisha Bello: cool. ping me when ready; I’ll sanity check alert noise before we go live.
