title: #eng-sre: 1770468009
preview:
Aisha Bello: Question for perf-canary alerting: do we want thresholds to be region/SKU-specific? Seeing eu-west p95 variance higher than us-east on same model, and H100 vs A100 behaves differently on tokens/sec.

Allison Grant: +1 we need to be careful. If we make alert rules key off region+sku+model+version we’re gonna explode config + paging complexity.

Sean Gallagher: The oncall pain is mostly: page fires in 1 region and people ask “is this real or just eu-west being eu-west”. If we keep global thresholds, we’ll either over-page in noisier regions or miss regressions in stable ones.

Rafael Mendes: What’s the actual delta we’re seeing? If it’s like +10-15% baseline variance in eu-west, we can bake it into warning thresholds. But for paging, I’d rather not have region-specific behavior unless there’s a strong reason.

Nadia Rahman: From baseline-results-week1, eu-west A100 pool has wider p95 spread (~1.25x IQR vs us-east). Also some queueing-time spikes correlate with fleet maintenance windows. So region does matter, but it’s not purely runtime.

Logan Wright: Telemetry side: if we add region+sku to every timeseries AND then make alerts per combo, it’s not just config; it’s metrics cost + retention. We can do it, but we need a small allowlist (top models) and a default fallback path.

Noah Patel: On canary service side, config already has region + sku as dimensions for target selection (PR-19226). The question is the evaluator + baseline store: do we fetch baseline keyed by (model, runtime, sku, region) or do hierarchical lookup.

Sofia Ivanova: Scheduler behavior: queueing time is VERY region/fleet dependent. Execution time is mostly sku dependent. If we want attribution, we probably need at least sku-specific baselines for runtime-only latency/tokens/sec.

Vanessa Ortiz: Fleet angle: eu-west is mixed stepping on A100s (don’t ask). Also some regions have more aggressive power caps. SKU label isn’t always enough unless we also have “fleet_profile”. But we should keep this sane.

Allison Grant: Proposal:
1) Baseline key hierarchy: (model, runtime_build, kernel_id, sku, region) -> fallback to (model, runtime_build, kernel_id, sku) -> fallback to (model, runtime_build, kernel_id)
2) Alert thresholds expressed as % delta from baseline, with an optional region multiplier
3) Only enable region-specific baselines for a small set: top-5 models + any model in “high-risk” list

Aisha Bello: That works for me. For paging we can still page on ANY region, but we’ll compare against the best baseline available for that region/sku so we don’t auto-page on “known noisy” regions.

Rafael Mendes: I like the hierarchical lookup. But we need a policy for “region baseline stale / missing” so we don’t silently fall back and then wonder why eu-west is paging. Can we include baseline_key_used in the alert payload?

Noah Patel: Yes. We already have payload fields for model/baseline/observed; can add `baseline_scope=region+sku|sku|global` to webhook (ties to pr-19244).

Logan Wright: If we do region-specific baselines, we should store them in the baseline artifact with explicit cardinality guardrails. Also make sure dashboards can filter by baseline_scope so folks can see when they’re on fallback.

Sean Gallagher: Another thing: who owns updating thresholds when we expand to a new region? This can’t be “SRE edits YAML at 2am”. Needs to be control plane / release checklist.

Connor O'Brien: From releases POV, we can add to the rollout checklist: “confirm region has baseline artifacts for target sku” and block enabling paging if baseline_scope would be fallback. We can make it a gate for phase-1 regions.

Nadia Rahman: Implementation detail: baseline artifacts currently get computed per (model, sku) from controlled runs. Adding region means we need controlled runs per region, which is more GPU. We can approximate region baseline as (sku baseline) + (region offset) measured from canary itself over longer windows.

Allison Grant: Careful: letting canary self-learn baseline is how we got baseline drift / alert storm last time. If we do offsets, they need to be fixed over a calibration window and then pinned.

Aisha Bello: Maybe this split:
- Baseline distributions remain per (model, runtime_build, kernel_id, sku)
- Region-specific config only tweaks alert thresholds (e.g., warn at +12% in eu-west vs +8% in us-east) OR require longer sustain window in noisier regions
- Add region-specific baselines only where we have known systematic differences AND we can afford the controlled run

Sean Gallagher: +1. Let’s not make region baselines the default. Do SKU baselines everywhere, and only region-special-case when we’ve proven it’s needed.

Rafael Mendes: Paging policy suggestion:
- Warn: region-aware (more tolerant in noisy regions), routes to #eng-runtime
- Page: consistent criteria across regions (sustained + high confidence), but uses sku baseline; require baseline_scope != global? (i.e., at least sku)

Noah Patel: I can make evaluator require sku baseline for paging. If only global baseline exists, we keep it at warning and include “missing sku baseline” in payload.

Logan Wright: That also helps cost/cardinality because we’re not trying to compute region baselines everywhere. We just need sku label to be reliable.

Vanessa Ortiz: SKU label reliability: we should use canonical hardware_sku from control plane (not whatever kube node label). I’ll sync with Connor on rollout config.

Allison Grant: Action items:
- Noah: add `baseline_scope` + “missing sku baseline” reason to alert payload; enforce sku baseline requirement for paging
- Aisha/Rafa: draft region-aware warning thresholds (eu-west, ap-south) + sustain window tweaks
- Logan/Nadia: confirm metrics labels stay the same; don’t add fleet_profile unless we absolutely need it
- Connor/Vanessa: update rollout checklist to verify sku baselines exist before enabling paging in a region

Aisha Bello: :thumbsup: I’ll start a doc snippet for the policy so we don’t keep re-litigating this every region expansion.

Sean Gallagher: Please also add a runbook note: “if baseline_scope=fallback, treat as low confidence” so oncall doesn’t panic.

Rafael Mendes: +1. Also, please make the Slack incident-bot message show baseline_scope in the first line. Small thing but helps triage.

Noah Patel: Ack. I’ll comment on incident-bot PR thread once the payload field is in.

Allison Grant: Cool. Let’s revisit after eu-west expansion week. If warnings are still noisy we can revisit region baselines for top-2 models only.
text:
Aisha Bello: Question for perf-canary alerting: do we want thresholds to be region/SKU-specific? Seeing eu-west p95 variance higher than us-east on same model, and H100 vs A100 behaves differently on tokens/sec.

Allison Grant: +1 we need to be careful. If we make alert rules key off region+sku+model+version we’re gonna explode config + paging complexity.

Sean Gallagher: The oncall pain is mostly: page fires in 1 region and people ask “is this real or just eu-west being eu-west”. If we keep global thresholds, we’ll either over-page in noisier regions or miss regressions in stable ones.

Rafael Mendes: What’s the actual delta we’re seeing? If it’s like +10-15% baseline variance in eu-west, we can bake it into warning thresholds. But for paging, I’d rather not have region-specific behavior unless there’s a strong reason.

Nadia Rahman: From baseline-results-week1, eu-west A100 pool has wider p95 spread (~1.25x IQR vs us-east). Also some queueing-time spikes correlate with fleet maintenance windows. So region does matter, but it’s not purely runtime.

Logan Wright: Telemetry side: if we add region+sku to every timeseries AND then make alerts per combo, it’s not just config; it’s metrics cost + retention. We can do it, but we need a small allowlist (top models) and a default fallback path.

Noah Patel: On canary service side, config already has region + sku as dimensions for target selection (PR-19226). The question is the evaluator + baseline store: do we fetch baseline keyed by (model, runtime, sku, region) or do hierarchical lookup.

Sofia Ivanova: Scheduler behavior: queueing time is VERY region/fleet dependent. Execution time is mostly sku dependent. If we want attribution, we probably need at least sku-specific baselines for runtime-only latency/tokens/sec.

Vanessa Ortiz: Fleet angle: eu-west is mixed stepping on A100s (don’t ask). Also some regions have more aggressive power caps. SKU label isn’t always enough unless we also have “fleet_profile”. But we should keep this sane.

Allison Grant: Proposal:
1) Baseline key hierarchy: (model, runtime_build, kernel_id, sku, region) -> fallback to (model, runtime_build, kernel_id, sku) -> fallback to (model, runtime_build, kernel_id)
2) Alert thresholds expressed as % delta from baseline, with an optional region multiplier
3) Only enable region-specific baselines for a small set: top-5 models + any model in “high-risk” list

Aisha Bello: That works for me. For paging we can still page on ANY region, but we’ll compare against the best baseline available for that region/sku so we don’t auto-page on “known noisy” regions.

Rafael Mendes: I like the hierarchical lookup. But we need a policy for “region baseline stale / missing” so we don’t silently fall back and then wonder why eu-west is paging. Can we include baseline_key_used in the alert payload?

Noah Patel: Yes. We already have payload fields for model/baseline/observed; can add `baseline_scope=region+sku|sku|global` to webhook (ties to pr-19244).

Logan Wright: If we do region-specific baselines, we should store them in the baseline artifact with explicit cardinality guardrails. Also make sure dashboards can filter by baseline_scope so folks can see when they’re on fallback.

Sean Gallagher: Another thing: who owns updating thresholds when we expand to a new region? This can’t be “SRE edits YAML at 2am”. Needs to be control plane / release checklist.

Connor O'Brien: From releases POV, we can add to the rollout checklist: “confirm region has baseline artifacts for target sku” and block enabling paging if baseline_scope would be fallback. We can make it a gate for phase-1 regions.

Nadia Rahman: Implementation detail: baseline artifacts currently get computed per (model, sku) from controlled runs. Adding region means we need controlled runs per region, which is more GPU. We can approximate region baseline as (sku baseline) + (region offset) measured from canary itself over longer windows.

Allison Grant: Careful: letting canary self-learn baseline is how we got baseline drift / alert storm last time. If we do offsets, they need to be fixed over a calibration window and then pinned.

Aisha Bello: Maybe this split:
- Baseline distributions remain per (model, runtime_build, kernel_id, sku)
- Region-specific config only tweaks alert thresholds (e.g., warn at +12% in eu-west vs +8% in us-east) OR require longer sustain window in noisier regions
- Add region-specific baselines only where we have known systematic differences AND we can afford the controlled run

Sean Gallagher: +1. Let’s not make region baselines the default. Do SKU baselines everywhere, and only region-special-case when we’ve proven it’s needed.

Rafael Mendes: Paging policy suggestion:
- Warn: region-aware (more tolerant in noisy regions), routes to #eng-runtime
- Page: consistent criteria across regions (sustained + high confidence), but uses sku baseline; require baseline_scope != global? (i.e., at least sku)

Noah Patel: I can make evaluator require sku baseline for paging. If only global baseline exists, we keep it at warning and include “missing sku baseline” in payload.

Logan Wright: That also helps cost/cardinality because we’re not trying to compute region baselines everywhere. We just need sku label to be reliable.

Vanessa Ortiz: SKU label reliability: we should use canonical hardware_sku from control plane (not whatever kube node label). I’ll sync with Connor on rollout config.

Allison Grant: Action items:
- Noah: add `baseline_scope` + “missing sku baseline” reason to alert payload; enforce sku baseline requirement for paging
- Aisha/Rafa: draft region-aware warning thresholds (eu-west, ap-south) + sustain window tweaks
- Logan/Nadia: confirm metrics labels stay the same; don’t add fleet_profile unless we absolutely need it
- Connor/Vanessa: update rollout checklist to verify sku baselines exist before enabling paging in a region

Aisha Bello: :thumbsup: I’ll start a doc snippet for the policy so we don’t keep re-litigating this every region expansion.

Sean Gallagher: Please also add a runbook note: “if baseline_scope=fallback, treat as low confidence” so oncall doesn’t panic.

Rafael Mendes: +1. Also, please make the Slack incident-bot message show baseline_scope in the first line. Small thing but helps triage.

Noah Patel: Ack. I’ll comment on incident-bot PR thread once the payload field is in.

Allison Grant: Cool. Let’s revisit after eu-west expansion week. If warnings are still noisy we can revisit region baselines for top-2 models only.

title: #eng-sre: 1770227766
preview:
PerfCanary: :rotating_light: perf-canary failure: `billing_meter_invariants` (run_id=pc-2026-02-24-0812)
Scenario: `streaming_retry_fallback_metering`
Result: FAILED (invariant violation: duplicate billed tokens)

Failure summary:
- expected `billed_completion_tokens` to be monotonic + equal to final completion length
- observed `billed_completion_tokens` increased twice for same `request_id` during server-side retry

Links:
- perf-canary PR (scenario): https://github.com/perf-canary/pr-912-add-billing-meter-regression-scenarios
- last green run: pc-2026-02-24-0640
- last deploy to canary: redwood@9c1f3a1 (includes PR-27555)

Log excerpt:
```
request_id=rwreq_8a2f... idempotency_key=idem_2b91...
chunk=7 usage_delta: +completion_tokens=128 +billed_completion_tokens=128
server_retry: upstream_timeout -> fallback_model=rw-llama-70b-instruct
chunk=1 usage_delta: +completion_tokens=64 +billed_completion_tokens=64   <-- should be 0 (retry)
final usage_total: completion_tokens=192 billed_completion_tokens=256
```

PagerDuty: [Triggered] perf-canary: billing meter invariant failure (severity=page) - eng-sre on-call

Aisha Bello: I’m on. Anyone else seeing pages? This looks like we regressed the retry/dedupe path again.

Leila Farouk: looking. That scenario is the one we added specifically to catch “double count on streaming retry/fallback” (same class as ENG-4430). The invariant is pretty strict: once a request retries, billed tokens must not increase for the retry attempt if it’s same user request.

Logan Wright: ugh. last deploy includes PR-27555 (idempotency hardening) and PR-27541 (region dim) iirc. The earlier fix for stream retry was PR-27463.

Nadia Rahman: can someone confirm whether the canary is hitting the OpenAI-compat endpoint or native Redwood endpoint? (token counting codepaths differ.)

Leila Farouk: native `/v1/chat/completions` (Redwood) but with streaming=true + simulated upstream timeout to force fallback.

Connor O'Brien: can we map this to a specific PR quickly? If it’s in canary only, we can halt rollout to prod.

Aisha Bello: checking deploy notes… canary picked up redwood@9c1f3a1 ~30m ago. That’s after merge of PR-27555-hardening-usage-event-idempotency.

Caleb Johnson: PR-27555 touched dedupe keys + dedupe window logic in the usage-event writer. Possible we’re emitting a second “usage_delta” event when retry kicks in.

Ethan Park: fallback routing is in control-plane; but metering attribution happens in the billing hooks. If dedupe key changed, retry attempt might look like a new event.

Aisha Bello: PerfCanary log says same `request_id` + same `idempotency_key`. So if we’re double counting, either:
1) dedupe key is not using idempotency_key anymore (bug)
2) streaming chunks are being treated as separate billable “finalize” events (bug)

Logan Wright: I’ll pull the code from PR-27555. There was a refactor from `dedupe_key = request_id + chunk_id` to include `attempt_id` so we can backfill safely. That might be the culprit: retry attempt increments `attempt_id` but should be excluded from billing totals.

Nadia Rahman: reminder: spec says server-side retries/fallback should be “no additional charge” when it’s transparent to customer (same user request). We should still record raw tokens for internal debugging, but billed_* must not increase.

Leila Farouk: yup. Canary checks `billed_*` only. It allows `raw_*` to increase on retry.

Aisha Bello: can someone run the canary scenario locally (or in staging) and confirm event stream? Need to see if it’s:
- duplicate usage events
- or a single usage event with wrong aggregation

Leila Farouk: I can repro in staging. Here’s the command the canary uses:
```
perf-canary run scenario streaming_retry_fallback_metering \
  --endpoint https://api-canary.redwood.ai \
  --model rw-llama-70b \
  --stream \
  --inject-upstream-timeout-at-chunk 7
```

Connor O'Brien: while that runs, I’m going to freeze rollout for any metering-related changes. Not pushing canary -> prod until we understand.

Aisha Bello: +1. Also, I’m going to silence the *secondary* drift alerts if they start flapping due to canary traffic. (We tuned this last week; shouldn’t page, but just in case.)

Caleb Johnson: quick data check from warehouse (canary account only). Seeing two finalize events?

Query:
```
select request_id, count(*) as n
from billing.usage_events
where request_id='rwreq_8a2f...'
  and event_type='finalize'
group by 1;
```
Result: n=2

Logan Wright: that’s it. Two finalize events for same request_id. Dedupe key is wrong.

Nadia Rahman: finalize should be exactly 1 per user request (per meter spec), regardless of retries. Retry attempts should be `debug_retry` / `attempt` records, not billable finalize.

Ethan Park: any chance control-plane is sending two “complete” signals? We do have retry handler in gateway + another in model-router.

Logan Wright: possible but dedupe should still collapse. PR-27555 changed dedupe window from 24h to 6h (to reduce storage), and also changed key composition. If it now includes `attempt_id`, then retry will bypass dedupe.

Aisha Bello: if dedupe key includes attempt_id, that’s a bug for billed meters. For raw telemetry, attempt_id is fine, but billed events must dedupe on (account_id, idempotency_key) at minimum.

Leila Farouk: staging repro confirms: I see two `finalize` writes, one per attempt. Second one has `billing_reason=retry_fallback` but it still increments billed tokens.

Leila Farouk: excerpt from staging logs:
```
usage_event_writer: emit finalize dedupe_key=acct:123|req:rwreq_8a2f|attempt:1
usage_event_writer: emit finalize dedupe_key=acct:123|req:rwreq_8a2f|attempt:2  <-- should be same key
```

Connor O'Brien: ok, so fix is straightforward: billed-finalize dedupe key must not include attempt_id. Might need to keep attempt_id for raw attempt events.

Logan Wright: I can patch quickly:
- billed finalize: `dedupe_key = account_id + idempotency_key` (fallback to request_id if missing)
- raw attempt: keep attempt_id in key
- add unit test for streaming+retry finalize dedupe

This is basically reintroducing the invariant that PR-27463 relied on.

Nadia Rahman: +1 and please ensure OpenAI-compat path uses same billed-finalize dedupe rules. We’ve had customers compare endpoints.

Aisha Bello: what’s the fastest mitigation for canary right now? If this is deployed to canary only, we can either revert PR-27555 or hotfix forward.

Connor O'Brien: revert is safer for immediate. But PR-27555 also had improvements for backfills (we want those). Hotfix forward is fine if small + well-tested.

Caleb Johnson: note: if canary is writing duplicate finalize events, that could leak into the recon exports, but it’s canary environment only. No RevRec impact. Still, we should treat as sev-2 bc it’s a regression in billing invariants.

Aisha Bello: agreed. Let’s do hotfix forward, and I’ll keep rollout frozen.

Logan Wright: I’ll open a PR now in redwood repo: “fix billed finalize dedupe key to exclude attempt_id” + add regression test mirroring perf-canary scenario.

Leila Farouk: please tag me. I’ll add an assertion in perf-canary to print both dedupe_key and attempt_id in failure output; makes future triage faster.

Nadia Rahman: also add a note in PR description: “billed meters must be stable under retries; raw meters may capture attempts.” Finance will ask.

Aisha Bello: drop links here when ready.

Logan Wright: PR up: https://github.com/redwood/pr-27602-fix-billed-finalize-dedupe-on-retry (draft)
Changes:
- `billed_finalize_dedupe_key = acct + idem_key` (fallback request_id)
- `raw_attempt_dedupe_key = acct + request_id + attempt_id`
- test: `TestStreamingRetryDoesNotDoubleBill()`

Connor O'Brien: reviewing. If we merge + deploy to canary, can we re-run perf-canary job manually?

Leila Farouk: yup. I can trigger `pc-run` once canary deploy completes.

Aisha Bello: while we’re here: should we add a guardrail in the writer: if we see a second finalize for same (acct, idem_key) in a short window, emit a WARN metric? That’d help catch before invoices.

Caleb Johnson: +1. Emit `metering_finalize_duplicate_detected` counter with labels (endpoint, model, region). That can page only if non-zero in prod.

Logan Wright: I can add in follow-up. Keeping this PR minimal for speed.

Nadia Rahman: ok minimal now, add guardrail next.

Connor O'Brien: merged. Deploying to canary now.

PagerDuty: [Acknowledged] perf-canary: billing meter invariant failure by Aisha Bello

PerfCanary: canary deploy detected: redwood@c4b8d20 (includes PR-27602)

Leila Farouk: rerunning scenario.

PerfCanary: perf-canary result: `billing_meter_invariants` PASSED
Scenario: `streaming_retry_fallback_metering`

Aisha Bello: :thumbsup: thanks all. Connor keep prod rollout frozen until we add the duplicate-finalize metric + quick audit of other codepaths?

Connor O'Brien: yep. I’ll schedule a short follow-up tomorrow with Logan/Nadia/Leila to confirm:
- OpenAI-compat endpoints covered
- embeddings batch metering not impacted
- any shadow-billing compare diffs expected

Logan Wright: I’ll open follow-up tickets:
1) emit `metering_finalize_duplicate_detected`
2) add a perf-canary scenario for OpenAI-compat streaming retry

Nadia Rahman: and I’ll update the metering spec appendix: “dedupe key requirements for billed meters” so we don’t repeat this.

Aisha Bello: closing the loop: incident summary (for thread)
- cause: PR-27555 changed dedupe key to include attempt_id -> retries emitted 2 finalize events
- impact: canary-only, would have overbilled in prod if shipped
- fix: PR-27602 exclude attempt_id from billed finalize dedupe key

Done.

title: #eng-sre: 1770183307
preview:
Connor O'Brien: heads up, need SRE eyes on proposed backfill plan + risk before we push the button.

Context: we have ~3.1% of Hosted API usage_events missing `cache_hit` + ~0.6% missing `region` over the last ~45 days (mostly from the ingestion retry window + one bad deploy). Product/Finance want the backfill so usage exports + experiment attribution stop being noisy.

Proposal (current):
- Run a replay job over `request_logs` -> regenerate usage events with the v2 mapper (same codepath as live)
- Write into `usage_events_v2_backfill` (partitioned by `event_day`)
- Dedupe using `usage_event_dedupe_key` (request_id + attempt_id + response_fingerprint)
- After backfill completes, flip the `usage_events_current` view to union (live v2 + backfill for historical partitions)

Ask: do we need to run shadow billing compare first? and what are the biggest "oops we changed billing numbers" risks here.

Aisha Bello: +1 on doing this carefully. immediate question: does anything in the backfill change `billed_tokens` or only fills dims (`region`, `cache_hit`)?

Connor O'Brien: the intent is dims-only. BUT the v2 mapper also includes the retry/fallback double-count fix (PR-27463) and the tool-call token attribution unit tests (PR-27471). so if we replay with v2 mapper, there *could* be token deltas in edge cases.

Mei Lin: that means it’s not “dims-only”, it’s “recompute canonical usage events”. which is fine, but it’s definitely billing-impacting if we ever use those recomputed events for ledger.

Logan Wright: yup. the backfill job is essentially the same pipeline as live. it will recompute:
- prompt_tokens / completion_tokens / billed_tokens
- cache_hit attribution
- region normalization
- retry path mapping

If we constrain the backfill to ONLY populate null fields, we’d need a custom patch job (and we’d still have to deal w/ how we decide which record is authoritative).

Nadia Rahman: strongly prefer not doing a patch-in-place. we’ve been burned with partial updates + mismatched semantics. if we’re going to fix, fix with canonical replay and then treat the result as `event_version=2`.

Emily Stone: Finance/RevRec POV: absolutely no retroactive invoice changes without explicit sign-off. Replaying historical usage into anything that the billing ledger reads is a big red flag.

If this backfill is ONLY for (a) customer usage exports and (b) experiment analysis tables, we can do it with guardrails. If it touches ledger inputs for already-issued invoices, we need (1) materiality analysis and (2) documented approval.

Aisha Bello: SRE POV: we should assume “billing-impacting until proven otherwise”. shadow compare seems required if v2 mapper changes token counts.

Also: what’s the blast radius + rollback? If `usage_events_current` flips, dashboards + exports will change immediately.

Connor O'Brien: rollback plan: keep view flip behind a feature flag in the warehouse (`use_usage_events_v2_backfill`). If we see anomalies, flip it off and we’re back to current.

Caleb Johnson: operational risk: replay will hammer Snowflake + the event enrichment workers. we should isolate the backfill compute (separate warehouse) and cap concurrency. Also watch duplicate rate.

Here’s the draft job config:
```yaml
job: usage-events-backfill-v2
range_days: 45
partitions: event_day
max_parallel_partitions: 6
max_retries: 3
write_table: usage_events_v2_backfill
emit_metrics: true
metrics:
  - duplicates_rate
  - null_region_rate
  - null_cache_hit_rate
  - billed_tokens_delta_vs_v1
```

Mei Lin: can we do this in 2 stages?
1) shadow billing compare first (no view flip), to quantify deltas
2) if deltas are within expectation + Finance signs off, then apply backfill + view flip for exports/analytics

And explicitly exclude closed periods / issued invoices from any ledger recompute.

Logan Wright: +1. Also, shadow compare should break down by:
- account_id
- model
- region
- endpoint (openai-compat vs native)
- streaming vs non-streaming

We’ve seen drift concentrate in openai-compat streaming.

Aisha Bello: what thresholds are we thinking? we need something SRE can page on during the run.

Connor O'Brien: proposal:
- page if `duplicates_rate > 0.05%` for any partition
- page if `billed_tokens_delta_vs_v1` > 0.25% overall OR > 1% for any top-20 account
- page if any partition freshness lag > 6h (job stuck)

(Mei/Logan sanity check?)

Mei Lin: 0.25% overall seems fine as an initial guardrail. for top accounts, 1% might still be a lot $$$. maybe add an absolute $ threshold too (e.g., >$2k delta in a day) once we map token deltas to rates.

Emily Stone: agree with absolute threshold. percent-only hides “big customer small %”.

Nadia Rahman: we can compute an estimated $ impact using current price sheet per account plan. won’t be perfect (discounts/credits), but good enough for alerting.

Caleb Johnson: for duplicates, watch both:
- duplicate dedupe_key within backfill output
- collisions between backfill + live events when the view unions

If we union by partition, we should ensure backfill partitions don’t overlap with live partitions post-cutover (or we do `QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER()` by version).

Connor O'Brien: good call. current plan is:
- for days in backfill range, `usage_events_current` will pick v2_backfill preferred
- live ingestion continues writing v2_live
- view logic:
```sql
SELECT * FROM (
  SELECT *, 2 AS event_version FROM usage_events_v2_backfill
  UNION ALL
  SELECT *, 2 AS event_version FROM usage_events_v2_live
  UNION ALL
  SELECT *, 1 AS event_version FROM usage_events_v1
)
QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
  PARTITION BY usage_event_dedupe_key
  ORDER BY event_version DESC, ingested_at DESC
)=1;
```

Aisha Bello: ok, that makes rollback easier too.

PagerDuty: (auto) Aisha Bello added "metering" tag to this thread.

Logan Wright: re: shadow compare — we already have `shadow_billing_compare` service (PR-27518) that computes old vs new meter output per request. We can run it against a sampled set (or full) for the last 7 days first.

If we see big deltas, we can isolate to retry/fallback scenarios.

Mei Lin: can someone paste the query we use for delta breakdown? want to make sure we’re consistent with Finance.

Caleb Johnson: this is the v1 snippet (can adapt):
```sql
WITH deltas AS (
  SELECT
    event_day,
    account_id,
    model,
    endpoint,
    SUM(billed_tokens_v1) AS bt_v1,
    SUM(billed_tokens_v2) AS bt_v2
  FROM billing_shadow_compare_daily
  WHERE event_day >= DATEADD('day', -7, CURRENT_DATE())
  GROUP BY 1,2,3,4
)
SELECT
  event_day,
  account_id,
  model,
  endpoint,
  bt_v1,
  bt_v2,
  (bt_v2 - bt_v1) AS delta_tokens,
  (bt_v2 - bt_v1) / NULLIF(bt_v1, 0) AS delta_pct
FROM deltas
ORDER BY ABS(delta_tokens) DESC
LIMIT 200;
```

Emily Stone: please ensure shadow compare outputs are retained + immutable for audit trail (at least through close). We’ll need evidence if we’re asked “why did usage exports change” even if invoices don’t.

Connor O'Brien: noted. we’ll write compare results to `billing_shadow_compare_daily` with run_id + code_sha.

Aisha Bello: suggestion for change control:
- run shadow compare for 72h on full traffic (or at least last 7d) BEFORE backfill
- then run backfill in prod but keep `use_usage_events_v2_backfill` flag OFF
- review deltas + SRE/Finance sign-off
- flip flag during low-traffic window

Also add a post-flip monitor for 24h.

Nadia Rahman: +1. also recommend we explicitly exclude the “usage freeze window” days for month-end close from any view flip until Finance says ok.

Mei Lin: which days are freeze? last thread said we freeze usage after T+3 days for close, right?

Emily Stone: correct. For Feb close, we’ll freeze through 2026-03-04 23:59 UTC (tentative). Anything affecting Feb numbers after freeze needs revrec approval + documented exception.

Connor O'Brien: ok, plan update:
1) Run shadow compare (full) for last 7 days tonight
2) Evaluate deltas w/ Mei + Emily tomorrow; if any top acct > $2k/day delta, we pause
3) Run backfill for last 45 days into `usage_events_v2_backfill`
4) Keep flag OFF through freeze window
5) After freeze + sign-offs, flip flag for exports/analytics only (ledger stays on current billing snapshots)

Aisha Bello: :thumbsup: from SRE. but I want a clear statement: ledger will NOT read `usage_events_current` directly for already-issued invoices.

Connor O'Brien: confirmed. ledger uses `billing_ledger_finalized` snapshots per invoice period. We are not regenerating those. This is about usage exports + experiment analysis tables.

Logan Wright: (reaction :white_check_mark:) also we should annotate usage exports: “usage definitions updated (event_version=2) as of <date>” so customers don’t think they’re being stealth-rebilled.

Emily Stone: +1, and Support/Sales should have the FAQ ready (billed vs raw tokens, caching, retries). Otherwise we’ll get tickets.

Mei Lin: last ask: can we ensure the backfill job has a kill switch mid-run? if duplicates spike, we should stop without corrupting partitions.

Caleb Johnson: we can write per-partition with atomic swap:
- write to `..._staging`
- validate counts/dupes
- then `ALTER TABLE SWAP` for that partition

Connor O'Brien: done. I’ll update the runbook + add the kill switch flag (`BACKFILL_ENABLED=false`).

Aisha Bello: cool. please link the runbook + dashboards in the deploy message before kickoff.

Connor O'Brien: will do. kickoff tomorrow 10am PT after we review shadow compare output.

Emily Stone: loop me on the shadow compare summary. if anything is material, we’ll take it to Laura (CFO) before proceeding.

Connor O'Brien: ack.

title: #eng-sre: 1770119025
preview:
Aisha Bello: need to lock down *freshness SLOs* for billing tables before we turn on paging. right now we have “it seems late” :grimacing:

Aisha Bello: context: Finance wants predictable lag for month-end + we need clear paging conditions + owners. can folks sanity check targets?

Mei Lin: +1. also need to be explicit what we measure: partition watermark vs max(event_time) is tricky w/ late arrivals.

Caleb Johnson: agreed. for pipelines we usually use `ingest_watermark_ts` (time the partition landed) not `event_time`.

Logan Wright: +1. event_time can be arbitrarily late w/ retries/backfills. SLO should be about “data available for billing aggregation” i.e. `pipeline_processed_ts`.

Emily Stone: Finance perspective: we don’t need real-time, but we do need *stable* daily totals for invoice preview + close. if it’s late, we need to know early + have an escalation path.

Aisha Bello: cool. proposing 2 layers:
1) operational freshness for day-to-day (alerts)
2) month-end “close readiness” gate (checklist + explicit freeze window)

Nadia Rahman: can we define the critical tables? i’d say:
- `usage_events_v1` (raw usage events)
- `usage_events_deduped_v1` (idempotency/dedupe applied)
- `billing_ledger_line_items_daily` (what invoicing reads)
- `customer_usage_export_daily` (what customers download)

Mei Lin: also the Finance recon export table we’re building (Snowflake) will be referenced by RevRec. that should get an SLO too.

Aisha Bello: yep. draft table list + targets below. pls nitpick.

Aisha Bello:
Proposed SLOs (all times are “age since expected partition end”):

A) `usage_events_v1` (hourly partitions)
- SLO: 99% of hourly partitions available within 15 minutes
- Notes: can be partial but should have “completeness flag”

B) `usage_events_deduped_v1` (hourly partitions)
- SLO: 99% within 30 minutes
- Rationale: allow for dedupe window + retry reconciliation

C) `billing_ledger_line_items_daily` (daily rollup)
- SLO: daily partition (UTC day) available by 05:00 UTC next day (T+5h)
- Stretch: 03:00 UTC

D) `finance_recon_export_v1` (daily, what Finance uses)
- SLO: available by 06:00 UTC next day (T+6h)
- Must include stable schema + rowcount check

E) `customer_usage_export_daily`
- SLO: available by 08:00 UTC next day (T+8h)
- Note: this is customer-facing, so lag needs comms if missed

Paging proposal:
- page if SLO miss persists > 2 consecutive intervals (2 hours for hourly tables; by 07:00 UTC for daily)
- warn (non-page) at 1 interval miss / approaching deadline

Ownership proposal:
- A/B owners: Telemetry (Logan/Nadia)
- C owner: Billing Hooks / Platform (Cole team) but SRE on-call gets alert
- D owner: Mei (data export) + Finance stakeholder Emily
- E owner: Platform + DX (usage API/export) (Ethan team)

Connor O'Brien: question: do we want 24/7 paging for A/B? if dedupe is late at 3am PT, it might self-heal.

Aisha Bello: good q. i think yes for “hard stop” windows (end of month, invoice preview). otherwise can be ticket-only.

Emily Stone: for month-end: we run invoice preview starting ~T+1 day and a couple times before send. if we don’t have the daily ledger by morning UTC, we slip.

Mei Lin: can we encode “business critical window”? like last 5 days of month + first 3 days of next month = paging. rest = Slack warn + Jira.

Logan Wright: i like that. keeps noise down. but we still want metrics to track chronic lateness.

Caleb Johnson: measurement details:
- hourly: compute `now() - max(pipeline_processed_ts)` for latest complete hour partition
- daily: `now() - expected_ready_ts(day)` where expected_ready_ts = day_end + 5h

Nadia Rahman: also need to define “complete partition” for A/B. raw usage events can be streaming in; we should mark partition complete once ingestion watermark passes + no backlog.

Caleb Johnson: we can use the existing `partition_state` table: `state in (COMPLETE, FINALIZED)`.

Aisha Bello: ok. then the SLO query should key off partition_state.

Connor O'Brien: what about late events/backfills? those will show up after partition FINALIZED.

Mei Lin: those should be separate from freshness SLO. i’d treat as correctness/drift checks. freshness SLO = “pipeline running”, not “no late events”.

Emily Stone: +1. late events are fine if they’re within policy and documented. but we need a cutoff for close.

Aisha Bello: month-end policy proposal (aligning w/ Finance thread):
- usage “freeze” for close: at T+48h after month end (UTC)
- after freeze, changes require Finance approval + documented backfill/audit trail
- exceptions: security/legal holds, or bug fix approved by CFO/RevRec

Logan Wright: freeze is consistent w/ our pipeline lag distribution. 48h covers long-tail retries.

Nadia Rahman: can we add an explicit “late event rate” metric to complement freshness? if late rate spikes, we might be hiding ingestion issues.

Aisha Bello: yes but that’s separate alert class (non-page unless billing drift > threshold). for this thread, let’s finalize freshness SLOs.

Caleb Johnson: re SLO numbers: 15m for A might be tight if we do cross-region compaction. but 99% should hold; p99.9 won’t.

Mei Lin: could do 30m for raw and 60m for deduped?

Logan Wright: i’d keep raw 15m (we need it for near-real-time sanity checks) and give deduped 45m.

Aisha Bello: compromise:
- A raw: 99% <= 20m
- B deduped: 99% <= 45m

Emily Stone: as long as daily ledger is ready by 05:00 UTC, Finance is happy.

Connor O'Brien: paging thresholds then:
- hourly: page if > 60m behind AND we’re in “billing critical window” (last 5 days of month through day 3)
- otherwise: #eng-sre warning + create Jira for owning team

Aisha Bello: works. for daily:
- warn at 04:30 UTC if ledger not present
- page at 06:00 UTC if still missing (always, not just close window)

Mei Lin: +1. daily ledger missing is always a fire.

Nadia Rahman: who owns the alert routing? we need labels so PagerDuty goes to the right rotation.

Aisha Bello: i’ll set up alerts in Grafana + PD routing based on `dataset` label:
- `usage_events_*` -> Telemetry on-call (Logan)
- `billing_ledger_*` -> Platform/Billing hooks on-call (Cole/Ethan rotation)
- SRE gets FYI + escalation if >2h unresolved

Logan Wright: ok. add runbook link w/ quick checks:
- check Airflow backlog
- check Kafka lag
- check dedupe job errors
- confirm no planned maintenance

Caleb Johnson: can include common commands:
`sql: select * from partition_state where table='usage_events_deduped_v1' order by hour desc limit 24;`

Aisha Bello: dropping a draft SLO-toolkit snippet so we can standardize:
```yaml
name: billing_dataset_freshness
service: metering-pipeline
indicator:
  type: sql_partition_freshness
  params:
    table: usage_events_deduped_v1
    partition_state_table: partition_state
    complete_states: ["COMPLETE","FINALIZED"]
objective:
  target: 0.99
  window: 30d
  freshness_threshold_minutes: 45
alerting:
  warn_after_minutes: 60
  page_after_minutes: 90
  page_only_in_billing_critical_window: true
owners:
  primary: telemetry
  secondary: eng-sre
```

Emily Stone: pls also note in runbook: if daily ledger misses, Finance needs heads-up in #finance within 30 mins with ETA.

Aisha Bello: yup, will add “Finance notify” step for C/D/E misses.

Mei Lin: last thing: timezone. everything should be UTC in the alert to avoid confusion.

Aisha Bello: agreed.

Connor O'Brien: ok to proceed. once alerts are live, we should do a 1-week “monitor only” (no paging) to tune.

Aisha Bello: good call. we’ll run in warn-only for 7 days except daily ledger (keep paging).

Logan Wright: :thumbsup:

Emily Stone: :thumbsup: thanks all

Aisha Bello: action items:
- Aisha: implement freshness SLO panels + alerts (warn-only for hourly for 7d; page on daily ledger)
- Mei: confirm expected_ready_ts for recon export + rowcount sanity check
- Logan/Nadia: provide runbook steps + known failure modes
- Connor: confirm “billing critical window” dates logic (calendar)

Aisha Bello: will post the Confluence link once it’s up.
text:
Aisha Bello: need to lock down *freshness SLOs* for billing tables before we turn on paging. right now we have “it seems late” :grimacing:

Aisha Bello: context: Finance wants predictable lag for month-end + we need clear paging conditions + owners. can folks sanity check targets?

Mei Lin: +1. also need to be explicit what we measure: partition watermark vs max(event_time) is tricky w/ late arrivals.

Caleb Johnson: agreed. for pipelines we usually use `ingest_watermark_ts` (time the partition landed) not `event_time`.

Logan Wright: +1. event_time can be arbitrarily late w/ retries/backfills. SLO should be about “data available for billing aggregation” i.e. `pipeline_processed_ts`.

Emily Stone: Finance perspective: we don’t need real-time, but we do need *stable* daily totals for invoice preview + close. if it’s late, we need to know early + have an escalation path.

Aisha Bello: cool. proposing 2 layers:
1) operational freshness for day-to-day (alerts)
2) month-end “close readiness” gate (checklist + explicit freeze window)

Nadia Rahman: can we define the critical tables? i’d say:
- `usage_events_v1` (raw usage events)
- `usage_events_deduped_v1` (idempotency/dedupe applied)
- `billing_ledger_line_items_daily` (what invoicing reads)
- `customer_usage_export_daily` (what customers download)

Mei Lin: also the Finance recon export table we’re building (Snowflake) will be referenced by RevRec. that should get an SLO too.

Aisha Bello: yep. draft table list + targets below. pls nitpick.

Aisha Bello:
Proposed SLOs (all times are “age since expected partition end”):

A) `usage_events_v1` (hourly partitions)
- SLO: 99% of hourly partitions available within 15 minutes
- Notes: can be partial but should have “completeness flag”

B) `usage_events_deduped_v1` (hourly partitions)
- SLO: 99% within 30 minutes
- Rationale: allow for dedupe window + retry reconciliation

C) `billing_ledger_line_items_daily` (daily rollup)
- SLO: daily partition (UTC day) available by 05:00 UTC next day (T+5h)
- Stretch: 03:00 UTC

D) `finance_recon_export_v1` (daily, what Finance uses)
- SLO: available by 06:00 UTC next day (T+6h)
- Must include stable schema + rowcount check

E) `customer_usage_export_daily`
- SLO: available by 08:00 UTC next day (T+8h)
- Note: this is customer-facing, so lag needs comms if missed

Paging proposal:
- page if SLO miss persists > 2 consecutive intervals (2 hours for hourly tables; by 07:00 UTC for daily)
- warn (non-page) at 1 interval miss / approaching deadline

Ownership proposal:
- A/B owners: Telemetry (Logan/Nadia)
- C owner: Billing Hooks / Platform (Cole team) but SRE on-call gets alert
- D owner: Mei (data export) + Finance stakeholder Emily
- E owner: Platform + DX (usage API/export) (Ethan team)

Connor O'Brien: question: do we want 24/7 paging for A/B? if dedupe is late at 3am PT, it might self-heal.

Aisha Bello: good q. i think yes for “hard stop” windows (end of month, invoice preview). otherwise can be ticket-only.

Emily Stone: for month-end: we run invoice preview starting ~T+1 day and a couple times before send. if we don’t have the daily ledger by morning UTC, we slip.

Mei Lin: can we encode “business critical window”? like last 5 days of month + first 3 days of next month = paging. rest = Slack warn + Jira.

Logan Wright: i like that. keeps noise down. but we still want metrics to track chronic lateness.

Caleb Johnson: measurement details:
- hourly: compute `now() - max(pipeline_processed_ts)` for latest complete hour partition
- daily: `now() - expected_ready_ts(day)` where expected_ready_ts = day_end + 5h

Nadia Rahman: also need to define “complete partition” for A/B. raw usage events can be streaming in; we should mark partition complete once ingestion watermark passes + no backlog.

Caleb Johnson: we can use the existing `partition_state` table: `state in (COMPLETE, FINALIZED)`.

Aisha Bello: ok. then the SLO query should key off partition_state.

Connor O'Brien: what about late events/backfills? those will show up after partition FINALIZED.

Mei Lin: those should be separate from freshness SLO. i’d treat as correctness/drift checks. freshness SLO = “pipeline running”, not “no late events”.

Emily Stone: +1. late events are fine if they’re within policy and documented. but we need a cutoff for close.

Aisha Bello: month-end policy proposal (aligning w/ Finance thread):
- usage “freeze” for close: at T+48h after month end (UTC)
- after freeze, changes require Finance approval + documented backfill/audit trail
- exceptions: security/legal holds, or bug fix approved by CFO/RevRec

Logan Wright: freeze is consistent w/ our pipeline lag distribution. 48h covers long-tail retries.

Nadia Rahman: can we add an explicit “late event rate” metric to complement freshness? if late rate spikes, we might be hiding ingestion issues.

Aisha Bello: yes but that’s separate alert class (non-page unless billing drift > threshold). for this thread, let’s finalize freshness SLOs.

Caleb Johnson: re SLO numbers: 15m for A might be tight if we do cross-region compaction. but 99% should hold; p99.9 won’t.

Mei Lin: could do 30m for raw and 60m for deduped?

Logan Wright: i’d keep raw 15m (we need it for near-real-time sanity checks) and give deduped 45m.

Aisha Bello: compromise:
- A raw: 99% <= 20m
- B deduped: 99% <= 45m

Emily Stone: as long as daily ledger is ready by 05:00 UTC, Finance is happy.

Connor O'Brien: paging thresholds then:
- hourly: page if > 60m behind AND we’re in “billing critical window” (last 5 days of month through day 3)
- otherwise: #eng-sre warning + create Jira for owning team

Aisha Bello: works. for daily:
- warn at 04:30 UTC if ledger not present
- page at 06:00 UTC if still missing (always, not just close window)

Mei Lin: +1. daily ledger missing is always a fire.

Nadia Rahman: who owns the alert routing? we need labels so PagerDuty goes to the right rotation.

Aisha Bello: i’ll set up alerts in Grafana + PD routing based on `dataset` label:
- `usage_events_*` -> Telemetry on-call (Logan)
- `billing_ledger_*` -> Platform/Billing hooks on-call (Cole/Ethan rotation)
- SRE gets FYI + escalation if >2h unresolved

Logan Wright: ok. add runbook link w/ quick checks:
- check Airflow backlog
- check Kafka lag
- check dedupe job errors
- confirm no planned maintenance

Caleb Johnson: can include common commands:
`sql: select * from partition_state where table='usage_events_deduped_v1' order by hour desc limit 24;`

Aisha Bello: dropping a draft SLO-toolkit snippet so we can standardize:
```yaml
name: billing_dataset_freshness
service: metering-pipeline
indicator:
  type: sql_partition_freshness
  params:
    table: usage_events_deduped_v1
    partition_state_table: partition_state
    complete_states: ["COMPLETE","FINALIZED"]
objective:
  target: 0.99
  window: 30d
  freshness_threshold_minutes: 45
alerting:
  warn_after_minutes: 60
  page_after_minutes: 90
  page_only_in_billing_critical_window: true
owners:
  primary: telemetry
  secondary: eng-sre
```

Emily Stone: pls also note in runbook: if daily ledger misses, Finance needs heads-up in #finance within 30 mins with ETA.

Aisha Bello: yup, will add “Finance notify” step for C/D/E misses.

Mei Lin: last thing: timezone. everything should be UTC in the alert to avoid confusion.

Aisha Bello: agreed.

Connor O'Brien: ok to proceed. once alerts are live, we should do a 1-week “monitor only” (no paging) to tune.

Aisha Bello: good call. we’ll run in warn-only for 7 days except daily ledger (keep paging).

Logan Wright: :thumbsup:

Emily Stone: :thumbsup: thanks all

Aisha Bello: action items:
- Aisha: implement freshness SLO panels + alerts (warn-only for hourly for 7d; page on daily ledger)
- Mei: confirm expected_ready_ts for recon export + rowcount sanity check
- Logan/Nadia: provide runbook steps + known failure modes
- Connor: confirm “billing critical window” dates logic (calendar)

Aisha Bello: will post the Confluence link once it’s up.

title: #eng-sre: 1770101888
preview:
Aisha Bello: heads up, meter drift alerts are paging *way* too often since we turned on the new by-account/model/region breakdown. I got 6 pages overnight, all < $20 impact and mostly late-event catchup. Can we tune thresholds today?

Grafana: Alert firing: `meter_drift_vs_ledger` (severity=page)
Labels: account=trial_9f2c, model=rw-llama-70b, region=us-east-1
Value: drift_pct=1.12%, drift_tokens=18,440

PagerDuty: [Triggered] Meter drift vs ledger (page) - eng-sre on-call

Logan Wright: +1, also saw it. But I don’t want to over-tune and miss real issues like the retry double-counting bug (ENG-4430) we just fixed.

Mei Lin: I pulled a quick sample from last night’s pages. 4/6 were caused by usage_events arriving ~90min late (warehouse lag), then the drift “self-healed” on next eval.

Caleb Johnson: yep ingestion was behind. snowpipe backlog after a schema deploy. caught up by ~03:10 PT.

Nadia Rahman: what’s the current rule logic? is it purely percent drift? we need abs guardrails.

Aisha Bello: current is:
- group by `account_id, model, region, day`
- compute drift_pct = abs(usage_tokens - ledger_tokens)/ledger_tokens
- page if drift_pct > 0.5% for 2 evals in a row
No abs threshold, no min-volume threshold.

Connor O'Brien: 0.5% is way too sensitive at low volume. Also we’re getting drift during shadow-billing compare runs + backfills; can we label/suppress those windows?

Ethan Park: also worth noting: after PR-27541 (missing region dimension fix), we have a couple days where ledger has region=unknown but events have region set. That’ll keep generating “drift by region” until backfill completes.

Mei Lin: +1. If ledger records didn’t have region, drift-by-region will always look bad even if totals match. We should either (a) alert on totals only until region backfill is done or (b) add an allowlist for known-incomplete dimensions.

Aisha Bello: proposal v1:
- keep *two* alerts:
  1) `meter_drift_total` (account/day, no region/model) => page
  2) `meter_drift_dimensional` (account/model/region/day) => slack warn unless big
- page criteria should be BOTH percent + absolute:
  - drift_pct >= 2.0%
  - AND drift_tokens >= 250,000 (or drift_usd >= 200)
- require ledger_tokens >= 1,000,000 to evaluate percent-based paging (avoid tiny denominators)
- add freshness gate: don’t evaluate drift if `usage_events_freshness_lag_minutes > 60`

Logan Wright: 2% + 250k tokens feels reasonable. 250k tokens is still “real” for most accounts. I’d like us to page if it’s clearly a double-counting path too (drift positive spikes). Can we include direction?

Aisha Bello: we can add a label for sign (events > ledger vs events < ledger). But I’m hesitant to treat direction differently in paging rules.

Nadia Rahman: direction matters for “we’re overbilling” vs “we’re underbilling” severity. For paging, overbilling is more urgent.

Mei Lin: can do:
- page if drift_pct >= 2% AND (drift_usd >= 200 OR drift_tokens >= 250k)
- BUT if events > ledger (potential overbill) lower drift_usd threshold to $100
That catches overbill quickly without paging for underbill noise.

Connor O'Brien: also add auto-silence during declared backfills. We already tag backfill jobs with `billing_mutation=true` in the pipeline metadata; can we pipe that into alert labels?

Caleb Johnson: we can. the drift query can join `metering_change_calendar` table we started for shadow billing + backfills (it’s in the recon dataset). Worst case we hardcode a config list for now.

Aisha Bello: ok. Here’s an updated rule sketch:

```
# Pseudocode
if freshness_lag_min > 60: skip
if in_backfill_window: warn_only
if ledger_tokens < 1_000_000: warn_only

drift_pct = abs(delta)/ledger

overbill = (usage_tokens > ledger_tokens)

page if drift_pct >= 0.02 and (
  drift_usd >= (overbill ? 100 : 200) or drift_tokens >= 250_000
)

warn if drift_pct >= 0.01 and drift_tokens >= 50_000
```

Logan Wright: +1. Can we also add an “N of M” condition so one bad eval doesn’t page? (late events can still slip through freshness gate sometimes.)

Aisha Bello: yep. make page require 2/3 evaluations over 30 min.

Mei Lin: for totals vs dimensional: I’d still like a total-drift page even when dimensional is silenced. That’s the one that maps closest to invoices.

Nadia Rahman: agree. totals is what Finance will ask about. dimensional can be noisy while we backfill missing dimensions.

Ethan Park: +1 on gating dimensional until the region backfill finishes. Otherwise we’ll train everyone to ignore the channel.

Connor O'Brien: can we add alert annotation linking to the runbook + “known incidents” section (e.g., region backfill ongoing)?

Aisha Bello: yep. I’ll add annotations:
- runbook: Confluence meter recon playbook
- link to `meter-sanity-dashboard` panel
- quick SQL snippet for last 24h drift

Caleb Johnson: also: the `freshness_lag` metric is per table, but drift query is hourly partitions. Might want to gate on “max event_time lag” instead of warehouse ingestion time.

Mei Lin: we have `max_event_timestamp` in the export table (from PR-27510). Can compute `now - max_event_ts` per account/day and use that.

Aisha Bello: perfect. I’ll change the gate to `event_time_lag_min > 90` to be safe.

Logan Wright: what about month-end close? there’s a freeze window where Finance wants stability; paging during that window is still valuable though (we want to know). Maybe warn-only but louder?

Mei Lin: during freeze we should avoid auto-remediation/backfills, but we still want signal. I’d keep paging if the impact is big (e.g., >= $1k).

Aisha Bello: ok, during freeze:
- page threshold becomes drift_usd >= 1000 (either direction)
- otherwise warn

Connor O'Brien: sounds good. who’s making the change? is it in the observability-pack repo or redwood?

Aisha Bello: observability-pack (grafana alert rules). I’ll open a PR w/ updated thresholds + annotations today, and we can monitor for 48h.

Grafana: Alert resolved: `meter_drift_vs_ledger` (severity=page)
Labels: account=trial_9f2c, model=rw-llama-70b, region=us-east-1

Nadia Rahman: please include a note in the PR description: “dimensional drift alert temporarily warn-only until region backfill complete” so nobody asks next week.

Aisha Bello: will do. also adding a panel showing “% of alerts suppressed due to backfill/freshness gate” so we can validate we’re not hiding real problems.

Logan Wright: :thumbsup: once merged, I’ll sanity check against the last 7 days of drift data to ensure we still would have caught the retry double-counting incident.

Mei Lin: I can run a backtest too using the recon export (last 30d) and post numbers in-thread.

Aisha Bello: action items:
- Aisha: PR w/ new thresholds + gating + annotations (EOD)
- Mei: backtest on 30d, confirm pages count + “would have caught” known incidents
- Logan: validate against ENG-4430 timeframe

Connor O'Brien: thanks. please also ping #finance once the page volume drops; they’ve been seeing the oncall noise spill into billing discussions.

Aisha Bello: will do.
